grades.question: What are the different user roles within FieldPulse?
grades.answer_key: Admin, Team Manager, Service Agent, Limited Agent, Assignment User
grades.student_response:
Admin
Team Manager
Service Agent
Limited Agent
Assignment

grades.question: At a high level, how would you describe how each of these user roles differ?
grades.answer_key: Admin users have access to everything. Team Managers and Service Agents have access to web & mobile app with configurable permissions toggled on/off by admins. Limited Agents can only access mobile app, can only upload files, use timesheets, be assigned to a team, can only see jobs directly assigned to them. Assignment Users have no access to web or mobile app, can be assigned to jobs/tasks/subtasks, receive email when assigned.
key_points: Team Manager/Service Agent = web & mobile with configurable permissions
Limited Agent = mobile only, limited visibility
Assignment User = no app access, email notifications only
grades.student_response:
Admin - highest level user, full permissions within the system.
Team Manager - more limited in their permissions, used for permissions more managerial in nature rather than administrative
Service Agent - classic technician role, no access to high level reporting, main focus on mobile app
Limited Agent - subcontractors who don't need full functionality in the system, for the most part this role is read only
Assignment User - can be used to keep track of internal equipment needed on jobs or for non-app subcontractors that only need to receive an email with job details

grades.question: What persona typically matches the Admin user role?
grades.answer_key: Owner, Office Admin
grades.student_response: Owner Operator or dedicated Office Manager
grades.comment: Partial answer. Has: Owner. Missing: Office Admin

grades.question: What persona typically matches the Team Manager user role?
grades.answer_key: Team Lead, Managers, Master Technicians
grades.student_response: Manager of a team, lower level office coordinator, high level technician

grades.question: What persona typically matches the Service Agent user role?
grades.answer_key: Technician

grades.question: What persona typically matches the Limited Agent user role?
grades.answer_key: Apprentice, contractor
grades.comment: Partial answer. Has: contractor. Missing: Apprentice

grades.question: What persona typically matches the Assignment User user role?
grades.answer_key: Subcontractors or even equipment/vehicles that the business might need to assign to jobs

grades.question: Can a FieldPulse customer create their own unique permissions?
grades.answer_key: NO! But they can toggle on/off the different available permission settings for each of their users
grades.comment: Got yes/no right but could add more context: can toggle on/off available permissions

grades.question: Can I give EACH individual technician access to a different Status Action Flow?
grades.answer_key: NO! Status Action Flows are created per role type not by each individual technician.
grades.comment: Got yes/no right but could add more context: per role type not individual

grades.question: Can a Service Agent view unscheduled/unassigned jobs in the mobile app?
grades.answer_key: NO!

grades.question: Can a Service Agent create Jobs assigned to themselves?
grades.answer_key: Yes, if the permission is toggled on in Company Settings.
grades.comment: Got yes/no right but could add more context: permission toggled on, Company Settings

grades.question: What are notable things that a Limited Agent User CANNOT do?
grades.answer_key: No access to web app, cannot set reminders, no Offline Mode, cannot open customer records from assigned Jobs, no customer list visibility, cannot change job status, No recurring jobs, No subtask visibility, No maintenance agreement visibility, No asset visibility, No financial visibility or ability to create these records
grades.student_response: Change Status of a Job, Access Web App, Open Customer Records, No Financial Visibility
grades.comment: Hit key points: 7/10

grades.question: Do Assignment Users get access to the FieldPulse platform? How do they know where to go?
grades.answer_key: No! They get an email outlining where they need to be, the time they need to be there, and any notes.
grades.student_response: No, they receive an email when assigned to a job
grades.comment: Missing key points: No access, location, time, notes

grades.question: As a service agent, what 3 fields cannot be filled out/adjusted on a Customer record in mobile?
grades.answer_key: Price Tiers, Net Due Dates for Estimates/Invoices, and Parent-Child relationship information.
grades.student_response: Service Agents - Price Tiers, Estimate Expiration Date/Invoice Due Date, Related Customer Info

grades.question: Can I create a new Customer record as I'm creating a Job record?
grades.answer_key: Yes.

grades.question: Can I create a new Customer record as I'm creating an Estimate/Invoice record?
grades.answer_key: Yes.

grades.question: Can I create a new Customer record as I'm creating a Project record?
grades.answer_key: Yes.

grades.question: Can I create a new Customer record as I'm creating an Asset record?
grades.answer_key: Yes.

grades.question: Can I create a new Customer record as I'm creating a Maintenance Agreement record?
grades.answer_key: Yes.

grades.question: What is the difference between Parent-Child relationships and Multi-Location/Multi-Contact?
grades.answer_key: Parent-Child: each account syncs separately to QB, flexible billing (bill to parent OR child). Multi-Location/Multi-Contact: Add multiple service locations and contacts under one OG customer record, only the main OG customer syncs to QB.
key_points: only main customer syncs to QB
grades.student_response: The parent child relationship will sync to QB, whereas the additional contacts and additional locations will not. The most common use case for parent child relationships is property management, where multi location would be better fit for a customer who has multiple residential properties.
grades.comment: Got some key points (2/4). Has: Parent-Child syncs separately to QB, bill parent or child. Missing: Multi-Location under one customer record, only main customer syncs to QB

grades.question: Do Net Due Dates on Parent automatically apply to Child?
grades.answer_key: The actual values don't transfer, but the 'rule' does. For example, if parent has estimates due in 15 days and invoices due in 90 days, child records will follow those same rules.
key_points: child follows parent's due date rules
grades.comment: Missing most key points: actual values don't transfer, rule/setting does transfer, child follows parent's due date rules

grades.question: Where do I find each customer's unique Customer Portal login link?
grades.answer_key: Customer Record > Actions button > Copy Customer Portal Link
grades.student_response: Customer Record > Actions > Copy Customer Portal Link

grades.question: Can a Customer Portal link be embedded into a Company's website?
grades.answer_key: NO!

grades.question: Can videos and pictures be viewed from the Customer Portal?
grades.answer_key: Yes!

grades.question: Can customers pay their Invoice records via the customer portal?
grades.answer_key: Yes, but only if the FieldPulse user has FieldPulse Payments enabled.
grades.comment: Got yes/no right but could add more context: FieldPulse Payments must be enabled

grades.question: In the customer portal, can a Parent customer see everything related to the Child customer?
grades.answer_key: Yes!

grades.section: General Knowledge: Projects, Jobs, Site Visits, & Tasks
grades.question: What is the difference between Projects, Jobs, and Site Visits?
grades.answer_key: Projects: folder that stores/organizes Job & Site Visit records, for long term work. Jobs: standalone or part of a Project, for work typically completed that day (standalone) or over several days/weeks (part of project). Site Visits: subcomponent of a Job record, for callbacks/go backs/return visits.
key_points: Jobs = standalone or part of project
Site Visits = subcomponent of Job for callbacks/return visits
grades.student_response:
Projects - High-level containers for long-term work that group multiple jobs, estimates, and invoices together.
Job - The specific scope of work or "work order" assigned to a customer to track progress and completion.
Site Visit - When a job requires a go back, or a specific job will last for less than a week and only needs one estimate and invoice.

grades.section: General Knowledge: Projects, Jobs, Site Visits, & Tasks
grades.question: What are the benefits of using Site Visit records instead of creating a brand new Job?
grades.answer_key: Quicker creation, directly attached to the primary work order, all information under one primary record. Don't have to duplicate a record leaving half info in one job and half in another.
grades.student_response: Quicker creation, directly attached to the primary work order, all information under one primary record as opposed to split in two

grades.section: General Knowledge: Projects, Jobs, Site Visits, & Tasks
grades.question: If you hear 'Call Back/Go Back/Follow Up' what record type do you recommend?
grades.answer_key: Site Visit

grades.section: General Knowledge: Projects, Jobs, Site Visits, & Tasks
grades.question: What is the difference between Job Notes & Field Notes?
grades.answer_key: Job Notes - carried over from Customer record and office staff adds info. Field Notes - where technicians dump their notes/findings while in the field.
key_points: Job Notes = from customer record/office staff
Field Notes = technician notes from the field
grades.student_response:
Job Notes - Office use to guide technicians and provide scope of work or other important details for the service
Field Notes - Where the technicians record the work performed to report back to the office
grades.comment: Partial answer. Has: Field Notes = technician notes from the field. Missing: Job Notes = from customer record/office staff

grades.section: General Knowledge: Projects, Jobs, Site Visits, & Tasks
grades.question: Can Job record Custom Fields change per each Job Template?
grades.answer_key: No.

grades.section: General Knowledge: Projects, Jobs, Site Visits, & Tasks
grades.question: Can I have multiple contacts listed on one Job record?
grades.answer_key: No, only one.
grades.comment: Got yes/no right but could add more context: only one

grades.section: General Knowledge: Projects, Jobs, Site Visits, & Tasks
grades.question: Can multiple Estimate and Invoice records be attached to one Job record?
grades.answer_key: Yes.

grades.section: General Knowledge: Projects, Jobs, Site Visits, & Tasks
grades.question: What can be attached to a Job Template?
grades.answer_key: Subtask, Material Lists, Estimate, Invoices, Forms, and Files.
grades.student_response: Subtasks, Material Lists, Estimates, Invoices, Forms, Files, Job Details

grades.section: General Knowledge: Projects, Jobs, Site Visits, & Tasks
grades.question: What feature for scheduling the most qualified technician? What needs to be done first?
grades.answer_key: The Find Availability feature on the Job record. User skillset tags have to be created in Company Settings first.
grades.student_response: Find Availability. User tags need to be setup first.

grades.section: General Knowledge: Projects, Jobs, Site Visits, & Tasks
grades.question: What is a Task in FieldPulse? Give 3 examples.
grades.answer_key: Tasks are non-billable events that appear on the calendar. Don't need to be assigned to any specific customer. Examples: PTO, internal meetings, lunch breaks, errands.
grades.student_response: Tasks are records used to block time off on the schedule that is not for a customer. Examples of a task would be: Internal Meeting, PTO, Sick Day
grades.comment: Some overlap but missing major points: non-billable events, on calendar, examples like PTO/meetings/lunch/errands

grades.section: General Knowledge: Projects, Jobs, Site Visits, & Tasks
grades.question: How does a Task get added to the schedule?
grades.answer_key: Create the Task Category in Company Settings. Then act as if creating a Job, but select Create Task. Complete the Task Category field, select team members, and date/time.
grades.student_response: Same process as adding a job, assign a date, time, and user(s)
grades.comment: Missing most key points: Task Category in Company Settings, Create Task instead of Job, Task Category field, team members, date/time

grades.question: ClearPath pitch in 4 sentences or less
grades.answer_key: With ClearPath, you, as a business owner, can customize the instructions and actions available to your technicians during each step of their workflow. This ensures that your technicians no longer question what to do or where to go within the mobile app; everything they need will be on one main screen.
grades.student_response: ClearPath provides the technicians out in the field with clear instructions and guidelines to complete any service, from start to finish, with desired actions performed. Things like clocking in and out, taking before or after photos, completing checklists, sending customer communications, creating estimates and invoices, and updating the status of a job. Take the performance of your best technician, and inject that into every other technician in the company, improving consistency of performance and significantly reducing the onboarding times of new hires.
grades.comment: Hit key points: 3/5

grades.question: List at least 3 value props of ClearPath.
grades.answer_key: Consistency for customers because each tech completes job same way; save time because guesswork removed; easier onboarding of new team members; save time with templates for Estimates and Forms; save time with default prepopulated text for comments.
grades.student_response:
- Consistency
- Customer Experience
- Capturing of Accurate Data
grades.comment: Missing most key points: save time/remove guesswork, easier onboarding, templates, prepopulated text

grades.question: Describe the relationship between Job Custom Status Workflows, Status Action Flows, and Focus View
grades.answer_key: Job Custom Status Workflows are the high-level Statuses common to a type of work. Status Action Flows allow the business to outline the steps and instructions for each specific job they perform (e.g., Shower Installation vs Toilet Installation). Focus View provides the custom mobile experience that neatly displays all of this information for each technician.
key_points: Status Action Flows = specific steps/instructions per job type
grades.student_response:
Job Custom Status Workflows - Highest level of workflow, determines the number of stages desired to take a service from start to finish.
Status Action Flows - Taking the structure of the Custom Status Workflow, and going more in depth. Determines the desired instructions, actions, and visibility for the technician within each stage of the workflow. 
Focus View - Really putting the 'horse blinders' on. Limiting the technicians visibility to solely the information the office wants to technician to have access to. Can be enabled but not enforced, where it can be opened or closed by swiping up or down on job record. Can also be enforced, where user cannot leave the view.
grades.comment: Hit key points: 2/3

grades.question: Are Action Buttons, Status Instructions, and Widgets all required at all times?
grades.answer_key: Nope! A customer can choose to just use Action Buttons and/or Status Instructions OR take it further by enabling Focus View and utilizing Widgets.
grades.comment: Got yes/no right but could add more context: can choose which to use, Widgets require Focus View

grades.question: Are technicians always locked into Focus View?
grades.answer_key: You can decide on a per Status basis when you want technicians to be locked into Focus View.
grades.comment: Partial answer. Has: No. Missing: per Status basis

grades.question: What can we create Custom Status Workflows for?
grades.answer_key: Job, Projects, Site Visits, Material Lists, Custom Forms, Estimates, and Invoices
grades.student_response: Jobs, Estimates, Invoices, Projects, Material Lists, Site Visits, Forms

grades.question: What are the benefits of some Status types in Custom Status Workflows?
grades.answer_key: Travel Time and In Progress status types track/log time. Pending status type pauses the time tracking.
grades.student_response:
On The Way - Tracks travel duration
In Progress - Tracks actual duration

grades.question: What two Status Types are required to save a Custom Status Workflow?
grades.answer_key: A 'New' and 'Completed' type of status are required.

grades.question: What are the 4 schedule views in FieldPulse?
grades.answer_key: List, Calendar, Dispatch, & Map view

grades.question: Can the Schedule be printed?
grades.answer_key: Yes.

grades.question: What does the Hourglass button on the Schedule do?
grades.answer_key: View all unscheduled and unassigned Jobs.
grades.student_response: Shows one of the following views: Unscheduled Jobs, Unassigned Jobs, Unscheduled & Unassigned Jobs, All Open Jobs

grades.question: How can someone view upcoming Jobs associated with a Maintenance Agreement?
grades.answer_key: Click Hourglass button, then in slide out window select Filter button, then in 'Is a Maintenance Agreement Job' field select 'Yes'.
grades.student_response: Maintenance Agreements > Open MA Record > Jobs
grades.comment: Missing most key points: Hourglass button, Filter button, Yes

grades.question: What does the '...' near the Hourglass button allow customers to do?
grades.answer_key: Adjust the color coding on the Schedule. Color coding can either mirror the Statuses of each Job OR each team member.
grades.student_response: Schedule Color View of either: "View By Status" or "View By Team Member Color"

grades.question: How does Route Optimization work? What are the limitations?
grades.answer_key: Schedule a Route or Reassign Jobs from a map view. Limitation is that this process is manual and not automated.
grades.student_response: You can either Schedule a Route or Reassign Jobs from a map view. The limitation is that this process is manual and not automated.

grades.question: How do I get access to the Booking Portal?
grades.answer_key: Must be enabled from Features & Plugins page. Then configure via Company Settings > Booking Portal tab.
grades.student_response: First, enable the feature (Company Settings > Features & Plugins > Booking Portal > Enable). Then, go to Company Settings > Booking Portal > Booking Portal URL.

grades.question: Do Booking Portal requests auto-create a new Customer record?
grades.answer_key: Yes.

grades.question: Can end customers attach pictures and videos to Booking Portal requests?
grades.answer_key: Yes, they can attach files, photos, and videos, but only if the FieldPulse user configured their Booking Portal that way.
grades.comment: Got yes/no right but could add more context: if configured by FieldPulse user

grades.question: How do I embed the Booking Portal on my website?
grades.answer_key: Embed the iFrame for your Booking Portal URL found in Company Settings > Booking Portal tab.
grades.student_response: Company Settings > Booking Portal > Booking Portal URL

grades.question: What is an Asset in FieldPulse? List 2 examples.
grades.answer_key: Asset records allow users to track information about assets they install and/or service for customers as well as track internal equipment. Examples: Water heater, employee computers/phones.
grades.student_response: Equipment that is installed or maintained by a FieldPulse user. Allows them to monitor things like condition, warranty information, and model information. Examples would be a generator or an AC unit.

grades.question: Why should we showcase Assets before jumping into Maintenance Agreements?
grades.answer_key: The flow is better on a live demo. On the Asset record we can show how to relate the Asset to other record types like Maintenance Agreements and Jobs.
grades.student_response: Maintenance Agreements are designed to manage the maintenance of a specific customer asset, so it's a natural progression of functionality
grades.comment: Missing key points: better demo flow, show relationship to other records, Jobs

grades.question: Can a Service Agent scan barcodes on assets?
grades.answer_key: Yes!

grades.question: What is a Maintenance Agreement in FieldPulse?
grades.answer_key: A Maintenance Agreement outlines the times a business will go out and perform maintenance on something they installed. Service frequencies typically quarterly or semi-annual. Contracts can be attached after the record has been created and saved.
grades.student_response: The record that manages the agreed upon maintenance of an asset, including both service and billing frequency, jobs that have been performed as a part of the agreement, and the associated piece of equipment that is being serviced.
grades.comment: Missing most key points: outlines maintenance schedule, service frequencies, contracts attached after creation

grades.question: Best practice for MA occurrence and billing creation?
grades.answer_key: All MA occurrences and billing details must live strictly under the MA record itself, not the origin Job or on an Estimate/Invoice record.
grades.student_response:
Occurence - Autogeneration from a pre-created job template
Billing - Unique instance of recurring billing created for that customer

Both will make this process more automated
grades.comment: Missing key points: under MA record itself, not on Estimate/Invoice

grades.question: Can multiple assets be listed on one invoice and billed together?
grades.answer_key: No.

grades.question: How can a technician review work order history for a specific asset?
grades.answer_key: From the Asset record, click the Related Jobs subtab.
grades.student_response: Navigate to the asset record, then to Related Jobs

grades.question: Two ways GPS coordinates can be used to find exact locations?
grades.answer_key: 1) Asset record GPS coordinates - attach coordinates to an asset for navigation. 2) GPS coordinates in Address Line 1 of Customer record - for properties without conventional addresses.
grades.student_response: The asset's location can be captured via a geolocated pin. The customer's/job's location itself can also be stored as a geolocated pin instead of a traditionally formatted address.
grades.comment: Got some key points (1/3). Has: Asset record GPS coordinates. Missing: Address Line 1 of Customer record, two distinct methods

grades.question: Is Recurring Jobs standalone or always tied to Maintenance Agreement?
grades.answer_key: Yes, Recurring Jobs is a standalone feature. Recurring Billing houses Invoice records for Recurring Jobs. Maintenance Agreements house Job records and Invoice records separately.
grades.comment: Partial answer. Has: standalone feature. Missing: not always tied to MA

grades.question: Is ACH supported in Recurring Billing?
grades.answer_key: No.

grades.question: Do we currently have Recurring Billing templates?
grades.answer_key: No.

grades.question: Can Recurring Billing be created in the mobile app?
grades.answer_key: No.

grades.question: Do saved credit cards pull into Recurring Billing?
grades.answer_key: Yes.
grades.comment: Expected: yes. Student answered: no

grades.question: How can Material Lists be used? Give two examples.
grades.answer_key: 1) Go List/Checklist for technician to load truck with needed tools/materials. 2) Pre Estimate Step - add items to Material List for billing, inventory deducted at Completed status, then pull items into Estimate or Invoice.
key_points: Pre Estimate step for billing items
grades.student_response:
Go List - provides the technicians with the equipments and parts necessary to complete a service, so they can pack their truck before going to the service site.
Logging - Keeping track of all parts used on site while on a job for the office.
grades.comment: Got some key points (1/3). Has: Go List/Checklist for loading truck. Missing: Pre Estimate step for billing items, two distinct examples

grades.question: What is a Line Item in FieldPulse?
grades.answer_key: A Line Item is anything customers need to complete a Job - parts, materials, labor and overhead.
grades.student_response: Any time or material item that a FieldPulse account would include on an estimate, invoice, material list, or PO
grades.comment: Missing key points: parts, labor, overhead, needed to complete a job

grades.question: How can we describe a Job/Service in terms of Estimate & Invoice creation?
grades.answer_key: A job/service is a collection of Line Items; what is needed to complete the job and charge the end customer.
key_points: what's needed to complete job and charge customer
grades.student_response: An estimate or invoice can be created directly from a job record, allowing for flexibility in workflows.
grades.comment: Missing key points: collection of Line Items, what's needed to complete job and charge customer

grades.question: What is the purpose of an Expiration Date on an Estimate?
grades.answer_key: Sets a time limit for how long quoted prices and terms are valid. Protects against price volatility. Encourages timely customer decisions.
grades.student_response: Encourages timely decision making for our customer's end users, and also protects the account from price fluctuations from suppliers
grades.comment: Got some key points (1/3). Has: encourages timely decisions. Missing: time limit for pricing, protects against price changes

grades.question: What are Bundles? Where do I create them?
grades.answer_key: Bundles are created in Company Settings and group together services and parts commonly added together. Save time by adding multiple items at once. Next best thing if not ready for Pricebook.
grades.student_response: Preconfigured groups of items to pull onto estimates, invoices, etc. They can be created via Company Settings > Estimates & Invoices > Invoice Bundles.
grades.comment: Missing key points: group services and parts, save time, alternative to Pricebook

grades.question: How many Estimate options can be added?
grades.answer_key: 30 Estimate options, but best practice is 3 (Good, Better, Best).
grades.student_response: I have attempted, and there doesn't appear to be a limit.
grades.comment: Missing key points: 30, best practice 3

grades.question: When is it crucial for a Contract to be attached to an Estimate?
grades.answer_key: When the user is looking to collect a signature on the Estimate in person/face-to-face with their customer.
grades.comment: Missing key points: collect signature, in person, face-to-face

grades.question: How many Contracts can be attached to an Estimate or Invoice?
grades.answer_key: Only one.
grades.comment: Partial answer. Has: 1. Missing: one

grades.question: Can every FieldPulse user request a down deposit on an Estimate?
grades.answer_key: MUST HAVE FIELDPULSE PAYMENTS ENABLED. On Draft Estimate click View Estimate, then Email to Customer, enter Payment Request Amount.
grades.comment: Missing most key points: must have FieldPulse Payments enabled, not every user, View Estimate, Email to Customer, Payment Request Amount

grades.question: Can a voice recording be attached to an Estimate or Invoice?
grades.answer_key: Yes.

grades.question: Can a user leverage different logos and branding from one Estimate to the next?
grades.answer_key: Yes.

grades.question: What limitations of child record estimate/invoice workflow cause problems?
grades.answer_key: The invoice remains tied to the child record, so you don't get true billing from parent to child. For proper parent-level billing the invoice must be created under the parent account.
key_points: invoice must be created under parent
grades.student_response: The parent cannot be directly billed for the child's invoice.
grades.comment: Missing most key points: invoice tied to child record, no true parent-to-child billing, invoice must be created under parent

grades.question: Difference between standard and Serialized Inventory tracking?
grades.answer_key: Standard tracks quantities of identical items in bulk. Serialized tracks each individual item with a unique serial number. Serialized has preconfigured Asset templates where serial number auto-fills.
grades.student_response: Standard inventory manages the general quantity of an inventory item, where serialized inventory manages the specific serialized instances of an inventory item.
grades.comment: Missing key points: standard = bulk quantities, serialized = unique identifier per item, serialized has asset templates

grades.question: Can Custom Fields be added to Inventory Line Items? Give examples.
grades.answer_key: Yes! Examples: Aisle, Shelf, Bin Number.
grades.comment: Partial answer. Has: Yes. Missing: examples like Aisle/Shelf/Bin Number

grades.question: When does Inventory get deducted?
grades.answer_key: Users select deduction preference: Material List (at Completed status) or Invoice (at Invoiced/Finalized status).
grades.student_response: Either a finalized invoice or completed material list

grades.question: Can a user decide which hub Inventory deducts from?
grades.answer_key: No, Inventory deducted from the Material List or Invoice creator's assigned hub every time.
grades.comment: Got yes/no right but could add more context: creator's assigned hub

grades.question: Can FieldPulse support truck-swapping technicians for Inventory?
grades.answer_key: No, doesn't track cross-truck usage in real time. Would be a huge manual lift, should not recommend.
grades.comment: Got yes/no right but could add more context: no real-time cross-truck tracking, huge manual lift

grades.question: How can supplier/vendor website links be saved for quick access?
grades.answer_key: Via Item List, add URL to Item Link section for each Line Item (Mobile) or click Add Link button next to Unit Cost (Web).
grades.student_response: When creating an item, a supplier link can be added via both the mobile and web app.

grades.question: Can FieldPulse validate inventory availability in real time?
grades.answer_key: No. Inventory levels are not checked in real time when adding items to estimates, invoices, or material lists.
grades.comment: Got yes/no right but could add more context: not checked in real time

grades.question: Best practice for reallocating materials after bulk shipment?
grades.answer_key: Manually move materials to trucks. Cannot have POs send inventory to multiple warehouses or trucks. Mobile: adjust quantities with +/- buttons per Hub. Web: similar manual adjustment.
grades.student_response: Order all new materials to the primary hub. Manually reallocate those items in the system from the main hub to the trucks as the items are pulled from the shelves.
grades.comment: Missing most key points: manual process, cannot auto-distribute from POs, mobile +/- buttons, web adjustment

grades.question: How many suppliers/vendors on one PO?
grades.answer_key: Only one.
grades.comment: Partial answer. Has: one. Missing: 1

grades.question: Does FieldPulse support partial receiving on a PO?
grades.answer_key: No!

grades.question: What is Supplier Chat? Value props?
grades.answer_key: Direct access to 8,500+ suppliers across 25 industries. Free feature. Can ask about inventory, pricing, order status, how to place an order. Can create PO and send via Supplier Chat. Available on web AND mobile.
grades.student_response: Supplier Chat is a messaging tool that allows your team to find local vendors and send Purchase Orders directly within FieldPulse to eliminate fragmented texts and emails.
grades.comment: Missing most key points: 8500+ suppliers, 25 industries, free feature, inventory/pricing/orders, web and mobile, send POs

grades.question: What Commission structures are available?
grades.answer_key: Company Default Commission, User-Specific Commission, Flat Rate Job Commissions (Pricebook), Ad-Hoc Commission.
grades.student_response: Company Wide or User Specific. Percentage of Gross Margin, Percentage of Gross Sales, Flat Dollar Amount.
grades.comment: Matched 1/4. Missing: Company Default, Flat Rate Job (Pricebook), Ad-Hoc

grades.question: Can Commission be split?
grades.answer_key: Yes, but at the moment this is a manual process, not automatic.
grades.comment: Got yes/no right but could add more context: manual process

grades.question: Can FieldPulse run payroll or integrate with payroll software?
grades.answer_key: No. Can bring in time but that's it. Export time and use outside of platform.
grades.comment: Got yes/no right but could add more context: can export time

grades.question: Value props of Pricebook? How explain to prospect?
grades.answer_key: Simple tool for technicians to create estimates/invoices quickly using preset flat-rate items and bundles. Build custom estimate/invoice on-site with tiered choices (Good/Better/Best). Present for signature and payment. Makes upselling easier, reduces quoting time, helps close jobs in the field.
grades.student_response: Flat rate tool that saves time quoting and invoicing by storing the selection of different flat rate services.
grades.comment: Got some key points (2/5). Has: flat-rate items, reduces quoting time. Missing: tiered choices Good/Better/Best, upselling, close in field

grades.question: What does flat rate pricing mean?
grades.answer_key: One set price for a job or service instead of billing by hour or by each part. Customer knows total cost upfront.
grades.student_response: The time and material are the same every time the service is performed

grades.question: Can Price Tiers be managed through Pricebook?

grades.question: What are the different user roles within FieldPulse?
grades.answer_key: Admin, Team Manager, Service Agent, Limited Agent, Assignment User
grades.student_response: Admin, Team Manager, Service Agent, Assignment, Limited Agent

grades.question: At a high level, how would you describe how each of these user roles differ?
grades.answer_key: Admin users have access to everything. Team Managers and Service Agents have access to web & mobile app with configurable permissions toggled on/off by admins. Limited Agents can only access mobile app, can only upload files, use timesheets, be assigned to a team, can only see jobs directly assigned to them. Assignment Users have no access to web or mobile app, can be assigned to jobs/tasks/subtasks, receive email when assigned.
key_points: Team Manager/Service Agent = web & mobile with configurable permissions
Limited Agent = mobile only, limited visibility
Assignment User = no app access, email notifications only
grades.student_response: Admins: Full access, They do not speak to prospects or customers, they are responsible for the administration of the CRM Platform of Fieldpulse and its access/functiionalites.  Team manager: Team manager, can make and view all customer data.  Create/assign jobs, Invoices, Estimates, Assets.  They have use of Pricebook and access to timesheets, as well as view internal activity and notes.  These permissions can be toggle on and off.  They DO NOT have access to company settings, or the ability to view other team's schedules.  Service Agent: Open Customer Profiles, view customer pages.  Create taks related to work, such as jobs and maintenance agreements and are assigned under a team manager if available.  Ability to view and edit most sales items and use functiions such as FP Payments, Pricebooks and Timesheets.  Cannot view Everyone's schedule, use reporting, set reminders or view company settings.  Assignment: They can be assigned to jobs, tasks and subtaks or receive an email when assigned to a job.  This must be enabled in Master.  They cannot access the FieldPulse App or use any Field Pulse function.  Limited Agent: Use the Mobile app only, they have a heavy restriction on job visibility and functions.  Ability to upload photos if enabled in Master.  Do not have access to the web app or ability to set reminders.  No financial or assett visibility and can only open customer records aassgined to their jobs.

grades.question: What persona typically matches the Admin user role?
grades.answer_key: Owner, Office Admin
grades.student_response: Owner or the Office administator or individual in charge of operations.  Dedicated financial or operational individuals may also fit this role if the company is large enough.

grades.question: What persona typically matches the Team Manager user role?
grades.answer_key: Team Lead, Managers, Master Technicians
grades.student_response: Lead Technician in charge of your teams, junior management perhaps, or a site leader if the company has multiple locations

grades.question: What persona typically matches the Service Agent user role?
grades.answer_key: Technician
grades.student_response: These would be your typical technicians or field service technician that want to have access to the standard functions for client interaction and job completions

grades.question: What persona typically matches the Limited Agent user role?
grades.answer_key: Apprentice, contractor
grades.student_response: This would be a technician that you would want to have limited by certain criteria such as the single job by job window.  They will be able to view and complete mostly just the work assigned to them, and have no access to the Web Platform as well as little access to anything outside of their assigned periphery.
grades.comment: Missing key points: Apprentice, contractor

grades.question: What persona typically matches the Assignment User user role?
grades.answer_key: Subcontractors or even equipment/vehicles that the business might need to assign to jobs
grades.student_response: This user is designed to receive an email outlining the job details. This user does not have access to the FieldPulse app or the ability to use any of its functionality. Contractors are a great example of an Assignment User.
grades.comment: Missing key points: equipment, vehicles

grades.question: What is the difference between Parent-Child relationships and Multi-Location/Multi-Contact?
grades.answer_key: Parent-Child: each account syncs separately to QB, flexible billing (bill to parent OR child). Multi-Location/Multi-Contact: Add multiple service locations and contacts under one OG customer record, only the main OG customer syncs to QB.
key_points: only main customer syncs to QB
grades.student_response: A Parent/Child relationship has the primary, top-level account as the main, which controls several child accounts that are individual service locations, units, or customers that are being serviced.  Multi Location lets you add multiple service addresses to a single primary customer record, and all service is tracked under the Original Customer Account.
grades.comment: Got some key points (2/4). Has: bill parent or child, Multi-Location under one customer record. Missing: Parent-Child syncs separately to QB, only main customer syncs to QB

grades.question: Do Net Due Dates on Parent automatically apply to Child?
grades.answer_key: The actual values don't transfer, but the 'rule' does. For example, if parent has estimates due in 15 days and invoices due in 90 days, child records will follow those same rules.
key_points: child follows parent's due date rules
grades.comment: Missing most key points: actual values don't transfer, rule/setting does transfer, child follows parent's due date rules

grades.question: Where do I find each customer's unique Customer Portal login link?

grades.question: Can a Customer Portal link be embedded into a Company's website?

grades.question: Can videos and pictures be viewed from the Customer Portal?

grades.question: Can customers pay their Invoice records via the customer portal?

grades.comment: Got yes/no right but could add more context: FieldPulse Payments must be enabled

grades.question: In the customer portal, can a Parent customer see everything related to the Child customer?
grades.answer_key: Yes!

grades.section: General Knowledge: Projects, Jobs, Site Visits, & Tasks
grades.question: What is the difference between Projects, Jobs, and Site Visits?
grades.answer_key: Projects: folder that stores/organizes Job & Site Visit records, for long term work. Jobs: standalone or part of a Project, for work typically completed that day (standalone) or over several days/weeks (part of project). Site Visits: subcomponent of a Job record, for callbacks/go backs/return visits.
key_points: Jobs = standalone or part of project
Site Visits = subcomponent of Job for callbacks/return visits
grades.student_response: Projects are the main overarching folder or data base that will organize and store all your subsequent Job & Site Visit records.  These are best used for Long term work & massive undertakings. Projects are ideal for work that may span weeks, months, or even years.  Jobs can be standalone records or part of a Project record. Best used for a standalone record: Work typically completed that day or as part of a Project record: Work can be completed over several days, weeks, or months. Think of these as individual appointments/stages/phases within the Project.  Site Visits are a sub section of the Job Record, and wil be their own job record.  These could be follow up calls or return visits after a job is completed.

grades.section: General Knowledge: Projects, Jobs, Site Visits, & Tasks
grades.question: What are the benefits of using Site Visit records instead of creating a brand new Job?
grades.answer_key: Quicker creation, directly attached to the primary work order, all information under one primary record. Don't have to duplicate a record leaving half info in one job and half in another.
grades.student_response: To keep the workflows as simple as possible since customers are tracking the overall progress of the job itself.  The site visits can add updates and visibility to even a signle job being worked.
grades.comment: Missing most key points: quicker creation, attached to primary work order, all info in one place, no duplication

grades.section: General Knowledge: Projects, Jobs, Site Visits, & Tasks
grades.question: If you hear 'Call Back/Go Back/Follow Up' what record type do you recommend?
grades.answer_key: Site Visit

grades.section: General Knowledge: Projects, Jobs, Site Visits, & Tasks
grades.question: What is the difference between Job Notes & Field Notes?
grades.answer_key: Job Notes - carried over from Customer record and office staff adds info. Field Notes - where technicians dump their notes/findings while in the field.
key_points: Job Notes = from customer record/office staff
Field Notes = technician notes from the field
grades.student_response: Job notes is a specific field for key information regarding the job.  Field Notes I would consider utilizing field notes.
grades.comment: Partial answer. Has: Field Notes = technician notes from the field. Missing: Job Notes = from customer record/office staff

grades.section: General Knowledge: Projects, Jobs, Site Visits, & Tasks

grades.section: General Knowledge: Projects, Jobs, Site Visits, & Tasks
grades.question: What is a Task in FieldPulse? Give 3 examples.
grades.answer_key: Tasks are non-billable events that appear on the calendar. Don't need to be assigned to any specific customer. Examples: PTO, internal meetings, lunch breaks, errands.
grades.student_response: A task can be something like a checklist before a job, a forced action to clock in, or anything else you want done as part of the job workflow mandatory.
grades.comment: Missing most key points: non-billable events, on calendar, not assigned to customer, examples like PTO/meetings/lunch/errands

grades.section: General Knowledge: Projects, Jobs, Site Visits, & Tasks
grades.question: How does a Task get added to the schedule?
grades.answer_key: Create the Task Category in Company Settings. Then act as if creating a Job, but select Create Task. Complete the Task Category field, select team members, and date/time.
grades.student_response: By creating the task you can then assign a task to the workflow.

grades.question: ClearPath pitch in 4 sentences or less
grades.answer_key: With ClearPath, you, as a business owner, can customize the instructions and actions available to your technicians during each step of their workflow. This ensures that your technicians no longer question what to do or where to go within the mobile app; everything they need will be on one main screen.
grades.student_response: Clearpath is a guided tour that your technicians can use to have a specific workflow like residential followed.  From there they can create jobs, material lists, and even bill the client directly.  This workflow can be created customed before hand, and then followed in the mobile app via clearpath.
grades.comment: Missing most key points: customize instructions/actions, per step of workflow, technicians know what to do, one main screen

grades.question: List at least 3 value props of ClearPath.
grades.answer_key: Consistency for customers because each tech completes job same way; save time because guesswork removed; easier onboarding of new team members; save time with templates for Estimates and Forms; save time with default prepopulated text for comments.
grades.student_response: The owner is able to set clear requests for their technicians in the order of operations.  It allows for certain guiderails to be put in place.  It gives the technician more ability to make requests and asks for payments via the mobile while on the job site.
grades.comment: Missing most key points: consistency, save time/remove guesswork, easier onboarding, templates, prepopulated text

grades.question: Describe the relationship between Job Custom Status Workflows, Status Action Flows, and Focus View
grades.answer_key: Job Custom Status Workflows are the high-level Statuses common to a type of work. Status Action Flows allow the business to outline the steps and instructions for each specific job they perform (e.g., Shower Installation vs Toilet Installation). Focus View provides the custom mobile experience that neatly displays all of this information for each technician.
key_points: Status Action Flows = specific steps/instructions per job type
grades.student_response: Job Custom Status workflow would be how the workflow for a specific job is set up for a pre designated pattern.  Status actions can be customized and give clear indicators on what stage a job is at in a given time.  The focus view would be a good way for you to view the schedule of the status action flows and what stage a job is in the workflow.
grades.comment: Hit key points: 2/3

grades.question: Are Action Buttons, Status Instructions, and Widgets all required at all times?
grades.answer_key: Nope! A customer can choose to just use Action Buttons and/or Status Instructions OR take it further by enabling Focus View and utilizing Widgets.
grades.comment: Got yes/no right but could add more context: can choose which to use, Widgets require Focus View

grades.question: Are technicians always locked into Focus View?
grades.answer_key: You can decide on a per Status basis when you want technicians to be locked into Focus View.
grades.student_response: No they are not always locked in to Focus View and yes you can change the statuses per technician.

grades.comment: Missing key points: Travel Time tracks time, In Progress tracks time, Pending pauses time tracking

grades.question: What is an Asset in FieldPulse? List 2 examples.
grades.answer_key: Asset records allow users to track information about assets they install and/or service for customers as well as track internal equipment. Examples: Water heater, employee computers/phones.
grades.student_response: An asset is a material piece of equipment with monetary value, such as a hot water heater or heavy HVAC system, probably installed at a client locaiton.

grades.question: Why should we showcase Assets before jumping into Maintenance Agreements?
grades.answer_key: The flow is better on a live demo. On the Asset record we can show how to relate the Asset to other record types like Maintenance Agreements and Jobs.

grades.question: What is a Maintenance Agreement in FieldPulse?
grades.answer_key: A Maintenance Agreement outlines the times a business will go out and perform maintenance on something they installed. Service frequencies typically quarterly or semi-annual. Contracts can be attached after the record has been created and saved.
grades.student_response: Maintenance agreements are reoccuring sales agreements between our clients and theirs on major pieces of the equipment.
grades.comment: Missing most key points: outlines maintenance schedule, service frequencies, contracts attached after creation

grades.question: Best practice for MA occurrence and billing creation?

grades.question: What is the purpose of an Expiration Date on an Estimate?
grades.answer_key: Sets a time limit for how long quoted prices and terms are valid. Protects against price volatility. Encourages timely customer decisions.
grades.student_response: Because the estimates should not have continuing life cycles in case products or pricing change.
grades.comment: Missing most key points: time limit for pricing, protects against price changes, encourages timely decisions

grades.question: What are Bundles? Where do I create them?

grades.student_response: Bundles are specific groupings of products which can also be set up as designated templates.
grades.comment: Missing key points: Company Settings, group services and parts, save time, alternative to Pricebook

grades.question: How many Contracts can be attached to an Estimate or Invoice?

grades.comment: Missing most key points: must have FieldPulse Payments enabled, not every user, View Estimate, Email to Customer, Payment Request Amount

grades.question: What limitations of child record estimate/invoice workflow cause problems?
grades.answer_key: The invoice remains tied to the child record, so you don't get true billing from parent to child. For proper parent-level billing the invoice must be created under the parent account.
key_points: invoice must be created under parent
grades.student_response: You would have to utilize the "Use separate Billing Address" to have it not default to the original billing.
grades.comment: Missing most key points: invoice tied to child record, no true parent-to-child billing, invoice must be created under parent

grades.question: Difference between standard and Serialized Inventory tracking?
grades.answer_key: Standard tracks quantities of identical items in bulk. Serialized tracks each individual item with a unique serial number. Serialized has preconfigured Asset templates where serial number auto-fills.
grades.student_response: Serializied Inventory enables detailed tracking of individual items by assigning unique serial numbers.  Serialized Inventory has unique ID numbers, specific hub locations, and preconfigured Asset templates.  These features are not available for Standard inventory tracking.

grades.question: Can Custom Fields be added to Inventory Line Items? Give examples.
grades.answer_key: Yes! Examples: Aisle, Shelf, Bin Number.
grades.student_response: Yes they can.  You could create one such as "Commercial" for a heavy piece of equipment or  a designation for a reoccuring maintenance request.
grades.comment: Partial answer. Has: Yes. Missing: examples like Aisle/Shelf/Bin Number

grades.question: When does Inventory get deducted?
grades.answer_key: Users select deduction preference: Material List (at Completed status) or Invoice (at Invoiced/Finalized status).
grades.student_response: When the status of the job is moved to completed.
grades.comment: Missing key points: Material List at Completed, Invoice at Invoiced/Finalized, user selects preference

grades.question: Can a user decide which hub Inventory deducts from?
grades.answer_key: No, Inventory deducted from the Material List or Invoice creator's assigned hub every time.
grades.comment: Expected: no. Student answered: yes

grades.question: Can FieldPulse support truck-swapping technicians for Inventory?

grades.question: How can supplier/vendor website links be saved for quick access?
grades.answer_key: Via Item List, add URL to Item Link section for each Line Item (Mobile) or click Add Link button next to Unit Cost (Web).
grades.student_response: Yes, and the suppliers that our clients already used can be found and added in the find supplier field.
grades.comment: Missing key points: Item List, Item Link section, mobile and web, Add Link button

grades.question: Can FieldPulse validate inventory availability in real time?
grades.answer_key: No. Inventory levels are not checked in real time when adding items to estimates, invoices, or material lists.
grades.comment: Expected: no. Student answered: yes

grades.question: Best practice for reallocating materials after bulk shipment?
grades.answer_key: Manually move materials to trucks. Cannot have POs send inventory to multiple warehouses or trucks. Mobile: adjust quantities with +/- buttons per Hub. Web: similar manual adjustment.
grades.student_response: You would set up the Hubs properly in Inventory Settings, make sure to enable multiple inventory hubs for all the trucks. Make sure that you have the right permissions to allow techs or site maangers to adjust inventory controls levels, while giving some view only.  Make sure that the correct users are added to the specific default inventory hubs.
grades.comment: Missing most key points: manual process, cannot auto-distribute from POs, mobile +/- buttons, web adjustment

grades.question: How many suppliers/vendors on one PO?
grades.answer_key: Only one.
grades.student_response: One supplier per purchase order in Fieldpulse system.
grades.comment: Partial answer. Has: one. Missing: 1

grades.question: Does FieldPulse support partial receiving on a PO?
grades.answer_key: No!
grades.comment: Expected: no. Student answered: yes

grades.question: What is Supplier Chat? Value props?
grades.answer_key: Direct access to 8,500+ suppliers across 25 industries. Free feature. Can ask about inventory, pricing, order status, how to place an order. Can create PO and send via Supplier Chat. Available on web AND mobile.
grades.student_response: It is a live interactive chat with the supplier which allows our customers to ask suppliers things like inventory count, pricing, and order status or update replacement.  You can also create Purchase orders here directly as well.
grades.comment: Missing most key points: 8500+ suppliers, 25 industries, free feature, inventory/pricing/orders, web and mobile, send POs

grades.question: What Commission structures are available?
grades.answer_key: Company Default Commission, User-Specific Commission, Flat Rate Job Commissions (Pricebook), Ad-Hoc Commission.
grades.student_response: Invoice level flat commissions, Margin of % or Margin of sales applied to the total, or manual commission adjustments.
grades.comment: Matched 0/4. Missing: Company Default, User-Specific, Flat Rate Job (Pricebook), Ad-Hoc

grades.question: Can Commission be split?
grades.answer_key: Yes, but at the moment this is a manual process, not automatic.
grades.comment: Got yes/no right but could add more context: manual process

grades.question: Can FieldPulse run payroll or integrate with payroll software?
grades.answer_key: No. Can bring in time but that's it. Export time and use outside of platform.
grades.comment: Expected: no. Student answered: yes

grades.question: Value props of Pricebook? How explain to prospect?
grades.answer_key: Simple tool for technicians to create estimates/invoices quickly using preset flat-rate items and bundles. Build custom estimate/invoice on-site with tiered choices (Good/Better/Best). Present for signature and payment. Makes upselling easier, reduces quoting time, helps close jobs in the field.
grades.student_response: Flat rate sales tool that helps supercharge the business sales process and increase revenue.  They can build and deploy thier own custom pricebook as well, creating tiered pricing with our Good, Better, Best scenario showcasing 3x choices.  Helps turns technicians into capable salesmen in the field.
grades.comment: Hit key points: 3/5

grades.question: What does flat rate pricing mean?
grades.answer_key: One set price for a job or service instead of billing by hour or by each part. Customer knows total cost upfront.
grades.student_response: Billing theod that charges a fixed upfront pricing for a product or service regardless of time and materials spent.
grades.comment: Missing key points: one set price, not hourly, customer knows cost upfront

grades.question: Can Price Tiers be managed through Pricebook?
grades.answer_key: No, Price Tiers are tied to Customer records not Pricebook.
grades.comment: Expected: no. Student answered: yes

grades.question: Can each Pricebook have different permissions per user?
grades.answer_key: No! Permissions cannot be set per pricebook. Access control applies to the Pricebook feature as a whole.

grades.question: What industries utilize Pricebook the most?
grades.answer_key: HVAC, Plumbing, Garage Door
grades.comment: Matched 1/3. Missing: Plumbing, Garage Door

grades.question: Main differences between Wisetack and Acorn Financing?
grades.answer_key: Wisetack: 3.9% convenience fee, $500-$25,000 loan range, FieldPulse user must apply (US based, 1 year in business, $150K revenue, 10+ reviews at 4.5+ stars). Acorn: No application for FieldPulse user, end customer applies directly, Acorn wires funds to end customer, $1,000-$100,000 loan range.
key_points: Acorn: no application, end customer applies, $1K-100K, funds to end customer
grades.student_response: These are both plugins at the company setting level.  Wisetac is the consumer side financing that we offer integrated for our clients to offer approved financings to their individual clients.  Acorn is the financing we are offering to our clients directly.  We currently do not offer Acorn Merchant financing to note.
grades.comment: Got some key points (1/2). Has: Acorn: no application, end customer applies, $1K-100K, funds to end customer. Missing: Wisetack: 3.9% fee, $500-25K, user must apply/qualify

grades.question: Which financing to recommend and when to offer?
grades.answer_key: Acorn Financing. Free for users, no application. 12+ lenders. Offer on Estimates (not Invoices) because funds take 1-2 business days. Customers may choose 'best' option since they have funding.
grades.student_response: We recommend using Acorn Finance when presenting Esimates instead of Invoices.  This is becuase it can take 1-2 business days for the funds to be directly deposited into the accounts borrower. This is also our default feature so there is no additional cost to you for this.
grades.comment: Got some key points (2/5). Has: Acorn, funds take 1-2 days. Missing: free, no application, offer on Estimates

grades.question: Can both Wisetack and Acorn be offered on Estimate records?
grades.answer_key: Nope! Must choose one or the other.

grades.question: Response to customer wanting to keep their own financing company?

grades.question: What is Card Fee Recovery (CFR)?

grades.question: What does CFR look like in FieldPulse?

grades.question: What are the different user roles within FieldPulse?
grades.answer_key: Admin, Team Manager, Service Agent, Limited Agent, Assignment User
grades.student_response: Team Manager, Service Agent, Limited Agent, Assignment User

grades.question: At a high level, how would you describe how each of these user roles differ?
grades.answer_key: Admin users have access to everything. Team Managers and Service Agents have access to web & mobile app with configurable permissions toggled on/off by admins. Limited Agents can only access mobile app, can only upload files, use timesheets, be assigned to a team, can only see jobs directly assigned to them. Assignment Users have no access to web or mobile app, can be assigned to jobs/tasks/subtasks, receive email when assigned.
key_points: Team Manager/Service Agent = web & mobile with configurable permissions
Limited Agent = mobile only, limited visibility
Assignment User = no app access, email notifications only
grades.student_response:
All permissions can be toggled on or off at the individual user-level. 

For those you want to provide the most functionality to, we would recommend that for your leaders, office admins, and potentially even a trusted senior technician. This would give those that need it all of the create, edit, and view permissions needed for their team in almost every part of the platform. 

For your technicians we recommend the Service Agent role. This would allow them to have meaningful interaction with their customers, giving them the customer history, warranty/asset information they need to provide great service every time. For those seasoned craftsman that your business depends on, those technicians that are great at the work, but absolutely hate technology, how do we get them bought in and using the platform? Meet ClearPath, a step-by-step guide that enables your most technology averse technicians to navigate with ease. 

Most of your team will typically fall into the Team Manager or Service Agent roles, but there will be times when you have temporary, or seasonal workers, and we have a role made specifically for them. This is our Limited Agent role. Mobile app only. Timesheets and assigned work order info along with any applicable notes or pictures. They can't mark a job complete, but they can upload before/after pictures and notes. Perfect for those limited agent roles our customers occasionally have. 

Then last would be our Assignment User. This is if you need to add someone to the work but they only need an email notification, we can make that person an Assignment User.

grades.section: General Knowledge: Projects, Jobs, Site Visits, & Tasks
grades.question: What is the difference between Projects, Jobs, and Site Visits?
grades.answer_key: Projects: folder that stores/organizes Job & Site Visit records, for long term work. Jobs: standalone or part of a Project, for work typically completed that day (standalone) or over several days/weeks (part of project). Site Visits: subcomponent of a Job record, for callbacks/go backs/return visits.
key_points: Jobs = standalone or part of project
Site Visits = subcomponent of Job for callbacks/return visits
grades.student_response:
Projects in FieldPulse are an organizational wrapper around several jobs. It's great for projects with simple billing. Simple billing like 1 Invoice = 1 Payment. If you need to house all of your work, or customer communication even, you can assign different jobs to different team members, and simplify the management of multi-job efforts. 

Jobs are the work that the technicians are doing. 1 job = 1 Invoice. This is the record of work and can be configured, tweaked a little, and viewed dynamically from different screens. 

Site visits are when a single job needs more than 1 trip out to the job site. This allows for better, more accurate numbers when looking at job profitability and first time fix rates. You can have multiple site visits on a single job, but this won't impact the invoice.
grades.comment: Hit key points: 2/3

grades.section: General Knowledge: Projects, Jobs, Site Visits, & Tasks
grades.question: What are the benefits of using Site Visit records instead of creating a brand new Job?
grades.answer_key: Quicker creation, directly attached to the primary work order, all information under one primary record. Don't have to duplicate a record leaving half info in one job and half in another.
grades.student_response: If you have planned return trips to the job site, but you don't need to create a new invoice for that return trip, job site visits are a great way to manage the schedule, without muddying the metrics.
grades.comment: Missing most key points: quicker creation, attached to primary work order, all info in one place, no duplication

grades.section: General Knowledge: Projects, Jobs, Site Visits, & Tasks
grades.question: If you hear 'Call Back/Go Back/Follow Up' what record type do you recommend?
grades.answer_key: Site Visit
grades.student_response: Job, Projects, Site Visit or Task, depending on the use case billing and need for unified customer communication.

Field Notes = technician notes from the field

grades.question: ClearPath pitch in 4 sentences or less
grades.answer_key: With ClearPath, you, as a business owner, can customize the instructions and actions available to your technicians during each step of their workflow. This ensures that your technicians no longer question what to do or where to go within the mobile app; everything they need will be on one main screen.
grades.student_response: How do you get your seasoned veteran technicians, those craftsman of the trade, but they hate technology; how do you get them to not only use but enjoy this app? That's exactly why we went back to the drawing board last year, we want this to function in the field for all of your guys. ClearPath is a simplified, clearly guided path for your technician to follow while in the mobile app. What once took 4+ clicks, is now just a single click, making the app easier to navigate when you are on the job site.
grades.comment: Got some key points (2/5). Has: technicians know what to do, mobile app. Missing: customize instructions/actions, per step of workflow, one main screen

grades.question: List at least 3 value props of ClearPath.
grades.answer_key: Consistency for customers because each tech completes job same way; save time because guesswork removed; easier onboarding of new team members; save time with templates for Estimates and Forms; save time with default prepopulated text for comments.
grades.student_response: Simplify onboarding and shorten ramp time. Ensure every service is ran just like your best tech would run it. Keep your technicians focused on the work, not getting lost in an app.
grades.comment: Missing most key points: consistency, save time/remove guesswork, easier onboarding, templates, prepopulated text

grades.question: Describe the relationship between Job Custom Status Workflows, Status Action Flows, and Focus View
grades.answer_key: Job Custom Status Workflows are the high-level Statuses common to a type of work. Status Action Flows allow the business to outline the steps and instructions for each specific job they perform (e.g., Shower Installation vs Toilet Installation). Focus View provides the custom mobile experience that neatly displays all of this information for each technician.
key_points: Status Action Flows = specific steps/instructions per job type
grades.student_response:
Job Custom Status Workflows are the different stages of the job. Would you run an install job just like a service? That's where custom status workflows for your jobs can come in handy. This is a customizable step-by-step workflow that can vary as much, or as little, as you need. This is a unique feature for FieldPulse that shows up regardless of the user, the type of platform they use to login, and enables complex jobs to be more simple, step-by-step instructions. 

Status Action Flows are even more granular steps for your mobile users. Think of the technician needing a before and after picture of the job site. Status Action Flow can provide this mobile technician an easier, more clearly spelled out guidance on the job itself to really ensure the technician isn't getting lost in the app. This can be turned on and off for different user roles, and only impacts the mobile experience. 

Focus View is an even tighter set of controls that will keep the technician exactly where they need to be for every single stage of the job. The customization here is impressive, if not daunting, but after getting this established your adoption rates will skyrocket.
grades.comment: Hit key points: 2/3

grades.question: Are Action Buttons, Status Instructions, and Widgets all required at all times?
grades.answer_key: Nope! A customer can choose to just use Action Buttons and/or Status Instructions OR take it further by enabling Focus View and utilizing Widgets.
grades.comment: Got yes/no right but could add more context: can choose which to use, Widgets require Focus View

grades.question: Are technicians always locked into Focus View?
grades.answer_key: You can decide on a per Status basis when you want technicians to be locked into Focus View.
grades.student_response: The "lock" is based on user roles per job, and is limited with only changing it broadly based on user role.
grades.comment: Missing key points: No, per Status basis

grades.student_response: Jobs, Site Visits, Projects, Estimates, Invoices, Material Lists, Purchase Orders, Agreements

grades.question: What are the benefits of some Status types in Custom Status Workflows?

grades.comment: Missing key points: Travel Time tracks time, In Progress tracks time, Pending pauses time tracking

grades.question: What two Status Types are required to save a Custom Status Workflow?
grades.answer_key: A 'New' and 'Completed' type of status are required.

grades.question: How does Route Optimization work? What are the limitations?
grades.answer_key: Schedule a Route or Reassign Jobs from a map view. Limitation is that this process is manual and not automated.
grades.student_response: You can filter down to date and team member, but beyond that there's not enough options to filter/order based on cost/job type/anything meaningful beyond a date. It's also difficult to share with a less than tech savvy technician, and would still require 3rd party gps apps to plot based on real-time traffic patterns and other real-world factors like travel wear-and-tear on the vehicle, gas, tolls, etc. The user has to click manually on the jobs, which are often difficult to see, leading to spending extra time searching, and then having to consider all of the mental modeling they have been doing on pen and paper, but now in a less than ideal setup for what they need.
grades.comment: Missing key points: Schedule Route or Reassign Jobs, map view, not automated

grades.question: How do I get access to the Booking Portal?

grades.question: Do Booking Portal requests auto-create a new Customer record?

grades.question: Can end customers attach pictures and videos to Booking Portal requests?

grades.comment: Got yes/no right but could add more context: if configured by FieldPulse user

grades.student_response: Again, they would need access to company settings, then booking portal, and then copy the Customer Portal Iframe Snippet Source Code and adjust the size needed to post on their site, and then work with their web developer, google/YouTube,  or AI it and get this posted on your site.

grades.question: What is an Asset in FieldPulse? List 2 examples.
grades.answer_key: Asset records allow users to track information about assets they install and/or service for customers as well as track internal equipment. Examples: Water heater, employee computers/phones.
grades.student_response:
An asset is a high value item. It can be installed at a customer's location, but for warranty purposes you need to track the location, condition, and service history for this asset, FieldPulse offers a great option for managing this asset. 

An asset is a high value item. It can be a tool, a test system, a high-value piece of equipment that is re-ordered, used for high-value jobs, that you need to track, keep the warranty front and center, or just a job critical item that the team needs. Our asset management tool allows you to track those internal, but still critical items with the same accuracy as those systems you've installed.

grades.question: Why should we showcase Assets before jumping into Maintenance Agreements?

grades.question: Can a Service Agent scan barcodes on assets?

grades.question: What is a Maintenance Agreement in FieldPulse?
grades.answer_key: A Maintenance Agreement outlines the times a business will go out and perform maintenance on something they installed. Service frequencies typically quarterly or semi-annual. Contracts can be attached after the record has been created and saved.
grades.student_response: FieldPulse is great for basic recurring billing, but it's not built for true recurring service ops or subscription style workflow.
grades.comment: Missing most key points: outlines maintenance schedule, service frequencies, contracts attached after creation

grades.comment: Missing key points: under MA record itself, not on Job, not on Estimate/Invoice

grades.comment: Partial answer. Has: Asset record. Missing: Related Jobs subtab

grades.question: Two ways GPS coordinates can be used to find exact locations?

You can tag an asset (from the mobile app) with a GPS coordinate that they can click on for future services. 

This can also be used for customer locations in-place of the common mailing address format as well. If they are currently using mapscos for their dispatching/routing, this might be preferable when making the switch.
grades.comment: Got some key points (1/3). Has: Asset record GPS coordinates. Missing: Address Line 1 of Customer record, two distinct methods

grades.question: Is Recurring Jobs standalone or always tied to Maintenance Agreement?

As the line items a service company would use to populate an estimate, and then add the labor manually or in a template. This would be for the materials you actually used on a job.

As the items needed to complete a job, from tools, parts, to supplies.

grades.question: What data cannot sync from QB Desktop?
grades.answer_key: Customer names >31 chars, special non-ASCII characters, line item names >31 chars, sales receipts, shipping fees, payments on estimates, additional payment methods beyond cash/check/credits/card/ACH, subclasses.
grades.student_response:
Customer

If the customer name exceeds 31 characters the sync will fail.

Special characters such as $,&,#, @, +, {,},: all can cause syncing issues.

Classes

Subclasses are currently not supported and will not sync.

Tax Codes

Multiple tax codes

Estimates

Payments on Estimates. It essentially leaves a ‘credit’ on the customer record in QuickBooks. This will show as ‘undeposited funds’ but not associated with any invoice or estimate.

Invoices

If the invoice item name exceeds 31 characters the sync will fail.

Special characters such as $,&,#, @, +, {,},: all can cause syncing issues.

Line Items

Negative line items currently not supported. This is currently considered a ‘discount’ in QuickBooks Desktop.

Credit Memos currently not supported.

Special characters such as $,&,#, @, +, {,},: all can cause syncing issues.

Sales receipts not currently supported.

Payment Method

Additional payment methods outside of cash, check, credit card or ACH are not supported. (Ex. gift card).

Others: QB receipts/grouping/FP Projects or Jobs.

grades.question: At a high level, how would you describe how each of these user roles differ?
grades.answer_key: Admin users have access to everything. Team Managers and Service Agents have access to web & mobile app with configurable permissions toggled on/off by admins. Limited Agents can only access mobile app, can only upload files, use timesheets, be assigned to a team, can only see jobs directly assigned to them. Assignment Users have no access to web or mobile app, can be assigned to jobs/tasks/subtasks, receive email when assigned.
key_points: Team Manager/Service Agent = web & mobile with configurable permissions
Limited Agent = mobile only, limited visibility
Assignment User = no app access, email notifications only
grades.student_response:
Admin access to everything
team manager doesnt have to be an actual manager but will have toned down functionality from an admin. more control into who and what info theyre looking at
service agent - intended for field guys who need to know where theyre going create est inv, take payment. able to hide the most from them 
lim agent - strictly for helpers and or subs - ONLY VIEW THEIR SCHEDULE, GENERAL BASIC NOTES, ADD PHOTOS, CLOCK IN OUT. NO MOBILE
assignment - placeholder, no login,
grades.comment: Got some key points (2/4). Has: Admin = full access, Limited Agent = mobile only, limited visibility. Missing: Team Manager/Service Agent = web & mobile with configurable permissions, Assignment User = no app access, email notifications only

grades.section: General Knowledge: Projects, Jobs, Site Visits, & Tasks
grades.question: What is the difference between Projects, Jobs, and Site Visits?
grades.answer_key: Projects: folder that stores/organizes Job & Site Visit records, for long term work. Jobs: standalone or part of a Project, for work typically completed that day (standalone) or over several days/weeks (part of project). Site Visits: subcomponent of a Job record, for callbacks/go backs/return visits.
key_points: Jobs = standalone or part of project
Site Visits = subcomponent of Job for callbacks/return visits
grades.student_response: Project is a bucket that related records and jobs can be put in . job is a calendar occurance that goes on the calendar at some point. site visit is another calendar occurance related to a job. (dont have to duplicate jobs)
grades.comment: Got some key points (1/3). Has: Site Visits = subcomponent of Job for callbacks/return visits. Missing: Projects = folder/organizer for long-term work, Jobs = standalone or part of project

grades.section: General Knowledge: Projects, Jobs, Site Visits, & Tasks
grades.question: What are the benefits of using Site Visit records instead of creating a brand new Job?
grades.answer_key: Quicker creation, directly attached to the primary work order, all information under one primary record. Don't have to duplicate a record leaving half info in one job and half in another.
grades.student_response: dont have to duplicate a record leaving half the info in one job and half the info in another. keeps things in one place
grades.comment: Some overlap but missing major points: quicker creation, attached to primary work order, no duplication

grades.question: ClearPath pitch in 4 sentences or less
grades.answer_key: With ClearPath, you, as a business owner, can customize the instructions and actions available to your technicians during each step of their workflow. This ensures that your technicians no longer question what to do or where to go within the mobile app; everything they need will be on one main screen.
grades.student_response: field techs shouldnt be expected to be masters of technology. need a way to create a company standard, guide them along the job in the way YOU want and ahave access to the correct info right infront of their face. CP does this. makes it idiot proof

grades.question: List at least 3 value props of ClearPath.
