grades.question: Describe the relationship between Job Custom Status Workflows, Status Action Flows, and Focus View
grades.answer_key: Job Custom Status Workflows are the high-level Statuses common to a type of work. Status Action Flows allow the business to outline the steps and instructions for each specific job they perform (e.g., Shower Installation vs Toilet Installation). Focus View provides the custom mobile experience that neatly displays all of this information for each technician.
key_points: Status Action Flows = specific steps/instructions per job type
grades.student_response:
job custom statuses are at the top, different stages 
status action flows are the different cp views that can be adjusted based on admin, manager, agent. 
focus view limits what is seen in the different action flows
grades.comment: Got some key points (1/3). Has: Focus View = custom mobile experience. Missing: Custom Status Workflows = high-level statuses, Status Action Flows = specific steps/instructions per job type

grades.question: What data cannot sync from QB Desktop?
grades.answer_key: Customer names >31 chars, special non-ASCII characters, line item names >31 chars, sales receipts, shipping fees, payments on estimates, additional payment methods beyond cash/check/credits/card/ACH, subclasses.
grades.student_response:
Customer

If the customer name exceeds 31 characters the sync will fail.  

Special characters such as $,&,#, @, +, {,},: all can cause syncing issues. 

 

Classes 

Subclasses are currently not supported and will not sync. 

 

Tax Codes 

Multiple tax codes 

 

Estimates 

Payments on Estimates. It essentially leaves a ‘credit’ on the customer record in QuickBooks. This will show as ‘undeposited funds’ but not associated with any invoice or estimate. 

Un-Selected Estimate Options

Groupings

 

Invoices 

If the invoice item name exceeds 31 characters the sync will fail.

Special characters such as $,&,#, @, +, {,},: all can cause syncing issues. 

Groupings

'Shipping' and 'Deposit' fields if they contain values

 

Line Items 

Negative line items currently not supported. This is currently considered a ‘discount’ in QuickBooks Desktop.

Credit Memos currently not supported.

Special characters such as $,&,#, @, +, {,},: all can cause syncing issues. 

Sales receipts not currently supported. 

 

Payment Method 

Additional payment methods outside of cash, check, credit card or ACH are not supported. (Ex. gift card).

 

Vendors  

This can be a tax code 

Suppliers

grades.question: At a high level, how would you describe how each of these user roles differ?
grades.answer_key: Admin users have access to everything. Team Managers and Service Agents have access to web & mobile app with configurable permissions toggled on/off by admins. Limited Agents can only access mobile app, can only upload files, use timesheets, be assigned to a team, can only see jobs directly assigned to them. Assignment Users have no access to web or mobile app, can be assigned to jobs/tasks/subtasks, receive email when assigned.
key_points: Team Manager/Service Agent = web & mobile with configurable permissions
Limited Agent = mobile only, limited visibility
Assignment User = no app access, email notifications only
grades.student_response:
Admin
Team Manager
Service Agent 

The 3 above are the main users in FP

Limited agent (typically used for 1099, subs, apprenteances, etc. Very limited and can only do 3 things. Do you know the 3 things? :) )

Assignement users (free users that cannot access the app via web or mobile. Just used for scheduling a piece of equipment, for example)

grades.section: General Knowledge: Projects, Jobs, Site Visits, & Tasks
grades.question: What is the difference between Projects, Jobs, and Site Visits?
grades.answer_key: Projects: folder that stores/organizes Job & Site Visit records, for long term work. Jobs: standalone or part of a Project, for work typically completed that day (standalone) or over several days/weeks (part of project). Site Visits: subcomponent of a Job record, for callbacks/go backs/return visits.
key_points: Jobs = standalone or part of project
Site Visits = subcomponent of Job for callbacks/return visits
grades.student_response:
Projects are a filin cabnit

Jobs are indivudual records that hold all job info like pics, files, videos, notes, estimates, invoices, etc

Site visits can be added for call backs to a job
grades.comment: Got some key points (1/3). Has: Site Visits = subcomponent of Job for callbacks/return visits. Missing: Projects = folder/organizer for long-term work, Jobs = standalone or part of project

grades.section: General Knowledge: Projects, Jobs, Site Visits, & Tasks
grades.question: What are the benefits of using Site Visit records instead of creating a brand new Job?
grades.answer_key: Quicker creation, directly attached to the primary work order, all information under one primary record. Don't have to duplicate a record leaving half info in one job and half in another.
grades.comment: Missing most key points: quicker creation, attached to primary work order, all info in one place, no duplication

grades.section: General Knowledge: Projects, Jobs, Site Visits, & Tasks
grades.question: If you hear 'Call Back/Go Back/Follow Up' what record type do you recommend?
grades.answer_key: Site Visit

grades.section: General Knowledge: Projects, Jobs, Site Visits, & Tasks
grades.question: What is the difference between Job Notes & Field Notes?
grades.answer_key: Job Notes - carried over from Customer record and office staff adds info. Field Notes - where technicians dump their notes/findings while in the field.
key_points: Job Notes = from customer record/office staff
Field Notes = technician notes from the field
grades.student_response:
job notes trasnfer to every work order for that customers

field notes are specific to that job
grades.comment: Similar but missing key specifics: Job Notes = from customer record/office staff, Field Notes = technician notes from the field

grades.section: General Knowledge: Projects, Jobs, Site Visits, & Tasks
grades.question: What is a Task in FieldPulse? Give 3 examples.
grades.answer_key: Tasks are non-billable events that appear on the calendar. Don't need to be assigned to any specific customer. Examples: PTO, internal meetings, lunch breaks, errands.
grades.comment: Missing most key points: non-billable events, on calendar, not assigned to customer, examples like PTO/meetings/lunch/errands

grades.section: General Knowledge: Projects, Jobs, Site Visits, & Tasks
grades.question: How does a Task get added to the schedule?
grades.answer_key: Create the Task Category in Company Settings. Then act as if creating a Job, but select Create Task. Complete the Task Category field, select team members, and date/time.
grades.student_response: from jobs on left menu or from the scheduling drop down choose task
grades.comment: Missing most key points: Task Category in Company Settings, Create Task instead of Job, Task Category field, team members, date/time

grades.question: ClearPath pitch in 4 sentences or less
grades.answer_key: With ClearPath, you, as a business owner, can customize the instructions and actions available to your technicians during each step of their workflow. This ensures that your technicians no longer question what to do or where to go within the mobile app; everything they need will be on one main screen.
grades.comment: Missing most key points: customize instructions/actions, per step of workflow, technicians know what to do, mobile app, one main screen

grades.question: List at least 3 value props of ClearPath.
grades.answer_key: Consistency for customers because each tech completes job same way; save time because guesswork removed; easier onboarding of new team members; save time with templates for Estimates and Forms; save time with default prepopulated text for comments.
grades.student_response:
SOPs

Easier training / onboarding for new hires

Getting all the info you need in the software while keeping no tech savvy techs happy
grades.comment: Missing most key points: consistency, save time/remove guesswork, templates, prepopulated text

grades.question: Describe the relationship between Job Custom Status Workflows, Status Action Flows, and Focus View
grades.answer_key: Job Custom Status Workflows are the high-level Statuses common to a type of work. Status Action Flows allow the business to outline the steps and instructions for each specific job they perform (e.g., Shower Installation vs Toilet Installation). Focus View provides the custom mobile experience that neatly displays all of this information for each technician.
key_points: Status Action Flows = specific steps/instructions per job type
grades.comment: Missing most key points: Custom Status Workflows = high-level statuses, Status Action Flows = specific steps/instructions per job type, Focus View = custom mobile experience

grades.question: Are Action Buttons, Status Instructions, and Widgets all required at all times?
grades.answer_key: Nope! A customer can choose to just use Action Buttons and/or Status Instructions OR take it further by enabling Focus View and utilizing Widgets.
grades.comment: Got yes/no right but could add more context: can choose which to use, Widgets require Focus View

grades.question: Are technicians always locked into Focus View?
grades.answer_key: You can decide on a per Status basis when you want technicians to be locked into Focus View.
grades.comment: Partial answer. Has: No. Missing: per Status basis

grades.answer_key: Job, Projects, Site Visits, Material Lists, Custom Forms, Estimates, and Invoices
grades.student_response: jobs, projects, material lists, and custom form
grades.comment: Matched 3/7. Missing: Site Visits, Custom Forms, Estimates, Invoices

grades.question: What are the benefits of some Status types in Custom Status Workflows?
grades.answer_key: Travel Time and In Progress status types track/log time. Pending status type pauses the time tracking.
grades.student_response: you can configure them to your workflow
grades.comment: Missing key points: In Progress tracks time, Pending pauses time tracking

grades.question: What two Status Types are required to save a Custom Status Workflow?
grades.answer_key: A 'New' and 'Completed' type of status are required.

grades.question: What is an Asset in FieldPulse? List 2 examples.
grades.answer_key: Asset records allow users to track information about assets they install and/or service for customers as well as track internal equipment. Examples: Water heater, employee computers/phones.
grades.student_response:
Equipment the customer install on their customers property

Water heater
New HVAC unit

grades.student_response: cuz you can link them and it paints the right picture
grades.comment: Missing key points: better demo flow, show relationship to other records, Maintenance Agreements, Jobs

grades.question: Can a Service Agent scan barcodes on assets?
grades.answer_key: Yes!

grades.question: What is a Maintenance Agreement in FieldPulse?
grades.answer_key: A Maintenance Agreement outlines the times a business will go out and perform maintenance on something they installed. Service frequencies typically quarterly or semi-annual. Contracts can be attached after the record has been created and saved.
grades.student_response: Helps customers keep track of their sold mainteances with details, reminders, and recurring billing via CC only. ACH soon. Homage Mo and Cookie Monster
grades.comment: Missing most key points: outlines maintenance schedule, service frequencies, contracts attached after creation

grades.question: Best practice for MA occurrence and billing creation?
grades.answer_key: All MA occurrences and billing details must live strictly under the MA record itself, not the origin Job or on an Estimate/Invoice record.
grades.comment: Missing key points: under MA record itself, not on Job, not on Estimate/Invoice

grades.question: Can multiple assets be listed on one invoice and billed together?

grades.question: Two ways GPS coordinates can be used to find exact locations?

grades.comment: Missing most key points: Asset record GPS coordinates, Address Line 1 of Customer record, two distinct methods

grades.question: Is Recurring Jobs standalone or always tied to Maintenance Agreement?
grades.answer_key: Yes, Recurring Jobs is a standalone feature. Recurring Billing houses Invoice records for Recurring Jobs. Maintenance Agreements house Job records and Invoice records separately.
grades.comment: Partial answer. Has: standalone feature. Missing: not always tied to MA

grades.question: Is ACH supported in Recurring Billing?

grades.question: How can Material Lists be used? Give two examples.
grades.answer_key: 1) Go List/Checklist for technician to load truck with needed tools/materials. 2) Pre Estimate Step - add items to Material List for billing, inventory deducted at Completed status, then pull items into Estimate or Invoice.
key_points: Pre Estimate step for billing items
grades.student_response: For example, you might list primary materials such as pipes, fittings, and fixtures, but you can also include tools like wrenches, pipe cutters, and plungers. Additionally, consider adding consumables such as Teflon tape, sealants, and joint compounds, as well as any specialized equipment needed for the installation, like pipe inspection cameras or drain snakes.
grades.comment: Missing most key points: Go List/Checklist for loading truck, Pre Estimate step for billing items, two distinct examples

grades.question: What is a Line Item in FieldPulse?
grades.answer_key: A Line Item is anything customers need to complete a Job - parts, materials, labor and overhead.
grades.student_response: an individual, detailed row entry within an estimate, invoice, or purchase order, representing a specific product, service, or expense
grades.comment: Missing key points: parts, materials, labor, overhead, needed to complete a job

key_points: what's needed to complete job and charge customer
grades.student_response: the work related to the revenue
grades.comment: Missing key points: collection of Line Items, what's needed to complete job and charge customer

grades.question: What is the purpose of an Expiration Date on an Estimate?
grades.answer_key: Sets a time limit for how long quoted prices and terms are valid. Protects against price volatility. Encourages timely customer decisions.

grades.comment: Missing most key points: time limit for pricing, protects against price changes, encourages timely decisions

grades.question: What are Bundles? Where do I create them?
grades.answer_key: Bundles are created in Company Settings and group together services and parts commonly added together. Save time by adding multiple items at once. Next best thing if not ready for Pricebook.

to insert groups of invoice items into an estimate or invoice.

company settings --> estimatews & invouices --> invoice bundles
grades.comment: Missing key points: group services and parts, save time, alternative to Pricebook

grades.question: How many Estimate options can be added?
grades.answer_key: 30 Estimate options, but best practice is 3 (Good, Better, Best).
grades.comment: Missing key points: 30, best practice 3

grades.question: When is it crucial for a Contract to be attached to an Estimate?
grades.answer_key: When the user is looking to collect a signature on the Estimate in person/face-to-face with their customer.

grades.question: What limitations of child record estimate/invoice workflow cause problems?
grades.answer_key: The invoice remains tied to the child record, so you don't get true billing from parent to child. For proper parent-level billing the invoice must be created under the parent account.
key_points: invoice must be created under parent
grades.student_response: they need it to show the child info too
grades.comment: Missing most key points: invoice tied to child record, no true parent-to-child billing, invoice must be created under parent

grades.question: Difference between standard and Serialized Inventory tracking?
grades.answer_key: Standard tracks quantities of identical items in bulk. Serialized tracks each individual item with a unique serial number. Serialized has preconfigured Asset templates where serial number auto-fills.
grades.student_response: Standard tracking tracks the total quantity of a specific item, while serialized inventory assigns a unique ID to every single unit
grades.comment: Missing key points: standard = bulk quantities, serialized has asset templates

grades.question: Can Custom Fields be added to Inventory Line Items? Give examples.
grades.answer_key: Yes! Examples: Aisle, Shelf, Bin Number.
grades.comment: Missing key points: Yes, examples like Aisle/Shelf/Bin Number

grades.question: When does Inventory get deducted?
grades.answer_key: Users select deduction preference: Material List (at Completed status) or Invoice (at Invoiced/Finalized status).

grades.comment: Missing key points: Material List at Completed, Invoice at Invoiced/Finalized, user selects preference

grades.question: Can a user decide which hub Inventory deducts from?

grades.comment: Got yes/no right but could add more context: creator's assigned hub

grades.question: Can FieldPulse support truck-swapping technicians for Inventory?

grades.comment: Got yes/no right but could add more context: not checked in real time

grades.question: Best practice for reallocating materials after bulk shipment?
grades.answer_key: Manually move materials to trucks. Cannot have POs send inventory to multiple warehouses or trucks. Mobile: adjust quantities with +/- buttons per Hub. Web: similar manual adjustment.
grades.student_response: id recoomend from inventory, searching the items, and updating the hubs
grades.comment: Missing most key points: manual process, cannot auto-distribute from POs, mobile +/- buttons, web adjustment

grades.question: How many suppliers/vendors on one PO?

grades.answer_key: Direct access to 8,500+ suppliers across 25 industries. Free feature. Can ask about inventory, pricing, order status, how to place an order. Can create PO and send via Supplier Chat. Available on web AND mobile.

grades.question: Value props of Pricebook? How explain to prospect?
grades.answer_key: Simple tool for technicians to create estimates/invoices quickly using preset flat-rate items and bundles. Build custom estimate/invoice on-site with tiered choices (Good/Better/Best). Present for signature and payment. Makes upselling easier, reduces quoting time, helps close jobs in the field.
grades.student_response:
It is a template that does NOT update real time, does NOT know your pricing, and you WILL have to update almost everything in it cuz its so outdated

I rarely sell it as it is currently a churn risk
grades.comment: Missing most key points: flat-rate items, tiered choices Good/Better/Best, upselling, reduces quoting time, close in field

grades.question: What does flat rate pricing mean?
grades.answer_key: One set price for a job or service instead of billing by hour or by each part. Customer knows total cost upfront.

grades.comment: Missing key points: not hourly, customer knows cost upfront

grades.question: Main differences between Wisetack and Acorn Financing?
grades.answer_key: Wisetack: 3.9% convenience fee, $500-$25,000 loan range, FieldPulse user must apply (US based, 1 year in business, $150K revenue, 10+ reviews at 4.5+ stars). Acorn: No application for FieldPulse user, end customer applies directly, Acorn wires funds to end customer, $1,000-$100,000 loan range.
key_points: Acorn: no application, end customer applies, $1K-100K, funds to end customer
grades.student_response:
Wisetack --> 4% dealer fee. money goes right to HVAC company

Acorn --> 0% dealer fee. Money goes to HVAC companys customer, then HVAC company
grades.comment: Got some key points (1/2). Has: Wisetack: 3.9% fee, $500-25K, user must apply/qualify. Missing: Acorn: no application, end customer applies, $1K-100K, funds to end customer

grades.question: Which financing to recommend and when to offer?
grades.answer_key: Acorn Financing. Free for users, no application. 12+ lenders. Offer on Estimates (not Invoices) because funds take 1-2 business days. Customers may choose 'best' option since they have funding.
grades.student_response: Acorn cuz anyone can get approved and higher financing limits
grades.comment: Missing most key points: free, no application, offer on Estimates, funds take 1-2 days

grades.question: Can both Wisetack and Acorn be offered on Estimate records?
grades.answer_key: Nope! Must choose one or the other.
grades.comment: Got yes/no right but could add more context: choose one

key_points: add link in Estimate email body
grades.student_response: No. you can use a custom field and hyper link it
grades.comment: Missing most key points: only native options are Wisetack/Acorn, add link in Estimate email body

grades.question: What is 10DLC compliance?
grades.answer_key: All numbers for SMS to U.S. numbers must be approved through The Campaign Registry (TCR). Industry-wide compliance program enforced by mobile carriers to reduce spam and protect consumers.
grades.comment: Missing most key points: Campaign Registry TCR, industry-wide, mobile carriers, reduce spam, protect consumers

grades.question: What is the two-step 10DLC compliance process?
grades.answer_key: 1) Brand Registration: submitting business details. 2) Campaign Registration: publishing SMS compliance pages on user's website (Facebook groups not accepted).
grades.student_response: The two-step 10DLC (10-Digit Long Code) compliance process requires businesses to register their identity and their messaging campaigns with The Campaign Registry (TCR), a centralized database that partners with US mobile carriers (AT&T, T-Mobile, Verizon) to verify senders. This process is essential for Application-to-Person (A2P) messaging to ensure high deliverability and avoid spam filters
grades.comment: Matched 1/3. Missing: Brand Registration, compliance pages on website

grades.question: What are the different user roles within FieldPulse?
grades.answer_key: Admin, Team Manager, Service Agent, Limited Agent, Assignment User
grades.student_response: Admin, Team Manager, Service Agent, Limited Agent and Assignment

grades.question: At a high level, how would you describe how each of these user roles differ?
grades.answer_key: Admin users have access to everything. Team Managers and Service Agents have access to web & mobile app with configurable permissions toggled on/off by admins. Limited Agents can only access mobile app, can only upload files, use timesheets, be assigned to a team, can only see jobs directly assigned to them. Assignment Users have no access to web or mobile app, can be assigned to jobs/tasks/subtasks, receive email when assigned.
key_points: Team Manager/Service Agent = web & mobile with configurable permissions
Limited Agent = mobile only, limited visibility
Assignment User = no app access, email notifications only
grades.student_response:
Admin is full access/full control
Team manager can only control their assigned team - no company settings or other teams. 
Service Agent - field techs - permissions can be adjusted as needed. 
Limited agent - view only their schedule, clock in/out, view only notes, upload photos. 
Assignment (never mentioned unless absolutely necessary) - No access to app or ability to use it.

grades.question: What persona typically matches the Admin user role?
grades.answer_key: Owner, Office Admin
grades.student_response: Owner/owners spouse, office admin that handles everything from calls to payment collection.

grades.question: What persona typically matches the Team Manager user role?

grades.student_response: Non owner, but manages techs, schedules work, handles quotes, invoices and payment collection.
grades.comment: Missing key points: Team Lead, Master Technicians

grades.student_response: Techs who do quotes, invoices and payment collection.
grades.comment: Missing key points: Technician

grades.answer_key: Apprentice, contractor

grades.section: General Knowledge: Projects, Jobs, Site Visits, & Tasks
grades.question: What is the difference between Projects, Jobs, and Site Visits?
grades.answer_key: Projects: folder that stores/organizes Job & Site Visit records, for long term work. Jobs: standalone or part of a Project, for work typically completed that day (standalone) or over several days/weeks (part of project). Site Visits: subcomponent of a Job record, for callbacks/go backs/return visits.
key_points: Jobs = standalone or part of project
Site Visits = subcomponent of Job for callbacks/return visits
grades.student_response:
Projects - longer-term with complex work assignments and specialized invoices 
Jobs - mostly one day or less, can be multiple days - simpler workflow and straight forward invoicing. 
Site visits - more than one trip to the site, or a next occurrence needed
grades.comment: Hit key points: 2/3

grades.section: General Knowledge: Projects, Jobs, Site Visits, & Tasks
grades.question: What are the benefits of using Site Visit records instead of creating a brand new Job?
grades.answer_key: Quicker creation, directly attached to the primary work order, all information under one primary record. Don't have to duplicate a record leaving half info in one job and half in another.

grades.question: ClearPath pitch in 4 sentences or less
grades.answer_key: With ClearPath, you, as a business owner, can customize the instructions and actions available to your technicians during each step of their workflow. This ensures that your technicians no longer question what to do or where to go within the mobile app; everything they need will be on one main screen.
grades.student_response: Do your techs ever call the office wondering what to do on a job or do you have a young tech fresh out of school who needs more guidance? We created a tool that dummy proofs the mobile app that can give you time back and ensure that your phone lines stay clear for more important calls.
grades.comment: Got some key points (2/5). Has: technicians know what to do, mobile app. Missing: customize instructions/actions, per step of workflow, one main screen

grades.question: List at least 3 value props of ClearPath.
grades.answer_key: Consistency for customers because each tech completes job same way; save time because guesswork removed; easier onboarding of new team members; save time with templates for Estimates and Forms; save time with default prepopulated text for comments.
grades.student_response: guardrails for techs, cleaner and easier to use for techs, ensures the work is done in a manner that the owner would be proud of.
grades.comment: Missing most key points: consistency, save time/remove guesswork, easier onboarding, templates, prepopulated text

grades.question: Describe the relationship between Job Custom Status Workflows, Status Action Flows, and Focus View
grades.answer_key: Job Custom Status Workflows are the high-level Statuses common to a type of work. Status Action Flows allow the business to outline the steps and instructions for each specific job they perform (e.g., Shower Installation vs Toilet Installation). Focus View provides the custom mobile experience that neatly displays all of this information for each technician.
key_points: Status Action Flows = specific steps/instructions per job type
grades.student_response:
JCSW - blue print 
SAF - step by step 
FV - guided experience
grades.comment: Missing most key points: Custom Status Workflows = high-level statuses, Status Action Flows = specific steps/instructions per job type, Focus View = custom mobile experience

grades.question: Main differences between Wisetack and Acorn Financing?

Acorn: No application for FieldPulse user, end customer applies directly, Acorn wires funds to end customer, $1,000-$100,000 loan range.

grades.question: At a high level, how would you describe how each of these user roles differ?
grades.answer_key: Admin users have access to everything. Team Managers and Service Agents have access to web & mobile app with configurable permissions toggled on/off by admins. Limited Agents can only access mobile app, can only upload files, use timesheets, be assigned to a team, can only see jobs directly assigned to them. Assignment Users have no access to web or mobile app, can be assigned to jobs/tasks/subtasks, receive email when assigned.
key_points: Team Manager/Service Agent = web & mobile with configurable permissions
Limited Agent = mobile only, limited visibility
Assignment User = no app access, email notifications only

grades.answer_key: Projects: folder that stores/organizes Job & Site Visit records, for long term work. Site Visits: subcomponent of a Job record, for callbacks/go backs/return visits.

grades.question: ClearPath pitch in 4 sentences or less
grades.answer_key: With ClearPath, you, as a business owner, can customize the instructions and actions available to your technicians during each step of their workflow. This ensures that your technicians no longer question what to do or where to go within the mobile app; everything they need will be on one main screen.
grades.student_response: from first arrival to final sign-off. Instead of relying on your techs to remember what to do next, ClearPath gives them specific action buttons, status instructions, and forms to complete at each step, so every job gets run the right way, every time. You can build separate workflows for each service type you offer — a Water Heater install looks different from an AC tune-up, and ClearPath treats them that way. The result is fewer mistakes, faster job completion, and the ability to scale your team without having to be on every job yourself.
grades.comment: Got some key points (2/5). Has: per step of workflow, technicians know what to do. Missing: customize instructions/actions, mobile app, one main screen

grades.question: List at least 3 value props of ClearPath.
grades.answer_key: Consistency for customers because each tech completes job same way; save time because guesswork removed; easier onboarding of new team members; save time with templates for Estimates and Forms; save time with default prepopulated text for comments.
grades.student_response:
1. Every tech runs the job your way, every time.

2. Office controls the field without being on every job.

3. One system, multiple service types.
You can build separate workflows for each job type
grades.comment: Missing most key points: consistency, save time/remove guesswork, easier onboarding, templates, prepopulated text

grades.question: Describe the relationship between Job Custom Status Workflows, Status Action Flows, and Focus View
grades.answer_key: Job Custom Status Workflows are the high-level Statuses common to a type of work. Status Action Flows allow the business to outline the steps and instructions for each specific job they perform (e.g., Shower Installation vs Toilet Installation). Focus View provides the custom mobile experience that neatly displays all of this information for each technician.

grades.student_response:
To build a report for technician call backs or service go backs over the last month, follow these steps:
Navigate to the Reporting section and select the ReportingController
Select the technicianSales report, which is specifically designed to compute performance metrics per technician
Apply a date range filter for the "last month" to capture the relevant period
Ensure the report includes the Call Backs metric, which identifies jobs tagged as "callback"
Alternatively, if you use Site Visit records for "Go Backs," filter your site visit reports by the "Service Go Back" status within your custom workflow
Review the generated table, which will display the Jobs Count, Call Backs, and Call Back Percentage for each technician

grades.question: What is the two-step 10DLC compliance process?

grades.student_response:
Step 1: Brand Registration (unchanged)

Customer submits business details (legal name, EIN, address, website, rep info).

We submit the Brand via TCR.

Step 2: Campaign Registration (new gating requirement)

Customer must publish two compliance pages on their website, then provide the URLs in Engage.

We submit the Campaign via TCR after URLs are provided.

grades.student_response:
Job records sync differently depending on the QuickBooks version:
QuickBooks Online (QBO)
Two-Way Sync: Customer records, which include job-related data, sync in both directions  
1
 
2
.
Operational Flow: FieldPulse acts as the operational system for jobs, scheduling, and invoicing, while QBO serves as the financial source of truth  
1
.
Invoices: Invoices created from jobs in FieldPulse sync to QBO with customers, line items, and notes  
1
.
QuickBooks Desktop (QBD)
Limited Sync: Job history, locations, and additional contacts do not transfer during the sync  
3
.
Manual Migration: Full data migration is typically required to bring over job history from QBD  
3
.
Web Connector: The sync is managed via the QuickBooks Web Connector, which must be installed and configured by an admin  
4
 
5
.

grades.question: What are the different user roles within FieldPulse?
grades.answer_key: Admin, Team Manager, Service Agent, Limited Agent, Assignment User
grades.student_response: Administrator,Team Manager, Service Agent, Limited agent, Assignment user

grades.question: At a high level, how would you describe how each of these user roles differ?
grades.answer_key: Admin users have access to everything. Team Managers and Service Agents have access to web & mobile app with configurable permissions toggled on/off by admins. Limited Agents can only access mobile app, can only upload files, use timesheets, be assigned to a team, can only see jobs directly assigned to them. Assignment Users have no access to web or mobile app, can be assigned to jobs/tasks/subtasks, receive email when assigned.
key_points: Team Manager/Service Agent = web & mobile with configurable permissions
Limited Agent = mobile only, limited visibility
Assignment User = no app access, email notifications only
grades.student_response: The Administrator has full access to the FieldPulse account and is best assigned to the Owner or office manager, The Team Manager will have access to most of the day to day operations but will not be able to view Company settings or other teams schedules, The Service agent will have access to the work assigned in the Field and is best for service techs, The limited agent can only access the mobile app and will only see jobs they are directly assigned to, and the assignment user is best described as a contractor and will only receive an email to an assigned job.

grades.question: What is the difference between Parent-Child relationships and Multi-Location/Multi-Contact?
grades.answer_key: Parent-Child: each account syncs separately to QB, flexible billing (bill to parent OR child). Multi-Location/Multi-Contact: Add multiple service locations and contacts under one OG customer record, only the main OG customer syncs to QB.
key_points: only main customer syncs to QB
grades.student_response: The Parent-Child relationship is a one main account that controls several smaller accounts like a corporate office or project management company when both entities need to be separate in quickbooks. Multi-location is when a commercial company has multiple locations but still a single primary customer record All service is tracked under original customer account and should be used to keep the Quickbooks customer list clean.

grades.question: Do Net Due Dates on Parent automatically apply to Child?
grades.answer_key: The actual values don't transfer, but the 'rule' does. For example, if parent has estimates due in 15 days and invoices due in 90 days, child records will follow those same rules.
key_points: child follows parent's due date rules

grades.section: General Knowledge: Projects, Jobs, Site Visits, & Tasks
grades.question: What is the difference between Projects, Jobs, and Site Visits?
grades.answer_key: Projects: folder that stores/organizes Job & Site Visit records, for long term work. Jobs: standalone or part of a Project, for work typically completed that day (standalone) or over several days/weeks (part of project). Site Visits: subcomponent of a Job record, for callbacks/go backs/return visits.
key_points: Jobs = standalone or part of project
Site Visits = subcomponent of Job for callbacks/return visits
grades.student_response: Projects would act as a folder that will store Long term work that spans weeks or months and stores jobs and site visits associated with the project, Jobs are standalone records or part of a project record best used for work completed over the course of days or weeks and site visits are a part of the individual job record-something like a callback or a return visit(Cannot be done in Service Titan/Workflows should be as simple as possible)

grades.section: General Knowledge: Projects, Jobs, Site Visits, & Tasks
grades.question: What are the benefits of using Site Visit records instead of creating a brand new Job?
grades.answer_key: Quicker creation, directly attached to the primary work order, all information under one primary record. Don't have to duplicate a record leaving half info in one job and half in another.
grades.student_response: Site visits would most likely not be getting invoiced from the customer, and can be tied to the specific job for future reference and reporting.
grades.comment: Missing most key points: quicker creation, attached to primary work order, all info in one place, no duplication

grades.section: General Knowledge: Projects, Jobs, Site Visits, & Tasks

Field Notes = technician notes from the field
grades.student_response: Job notes refer to notes required for the initial job while the field notes are added in case there is a Call back or a go back(Site Visit)

grades.question: ClearPath pitch in 4 sentences or less
grades.answer_key: With ClearPath, you, as a business owner, can customize the instructions and actions available to your technicians during each step of their workflow. This ensures that your technicians no longer question what to do or where to go within the mobile app; everything they need will be on one main screen.
grades.student_response: ClearPath is a way to customize instructions and actions for your techs during each step of the workflow. This ensures that techs know what to do next and where to go within the app within one screen. It keeps techs consistent across the board, save time per job, and makes training and adoption of the platform seamless. It is in place to make sure that essential tasks like clocking in and taking pictures are handled effectively throughout each job.

grades.question: List at least 3 value props of ClearPath.
grades.answer_key: Consistency for customers because each tech completes job same way; save time because guesswork removed; easier onboarding of new team members; save time with templates for Estimates and Forms; save time with default prepopulated text for comments.
grades.student_response: By having all the information in one place techs can complete jobs more quickly leading to more jobs per day, ClearPath ensures that every job is completed to your standard regardless of tech and really leans into a positive experience for your customers so that recurring business is a part of your everyday, Your technicians will find it simpler to learn the platform making onboarding and training that much smoother so that techs feel confident in the platform leading to less fewer errors and a better experience for your team and customers.
grades.comment: Missing most key points: consistency, save time/remove guesswork, easier onboarding, templates, prepopulated text

grades.question: Describe the relationship between Job Custom Status Workflows, Status Action Flows, and Focus View
grades.answer_key: Job Custom Status Workflows are the high-level Statuses common to a type of work. Status Action Flows allow the business to outline the steps and instructions for each specific job they perform (e.g., Shower Installation vs Toilet Installation). Focus View provides the custom mobile experience that neatly displays all of this information for each technician.
key_points: Status Action Flows = specific steps/instructions per job type
grades.student_response: Custom Status workflows are the overall blueprint for the type of work or job, Status Action Flows are the steps to the job or service you are providing, the Focus View is the guided experience acting as guard rails so that jobs are completed the same way every single time.
grades.comment: Hit key points: 2/3

grades.question: Are Action Buttons, Status Instructions, and Widgets all required at all times?

grades.comment: Got yes/no right but could add more context: can choose which to use, Widgets require Focus View

grades.comment: Partial answer. Has: No. Missing: per Status basis

grades.question: How does Route Optimization work? What are the limitations?
grades.answer_key: Schedule a Route or Reassign Jobs from a map view. Limitation is that this process is manual and not automated.
grades.student_response:
We can Assign jobs close to one another to the same team member by viewing them on the Map and then use the Schedule route action and select the order in which we would like the team member to complete them. Then we can select when that first job takes place and the default length and duration of each job. Then we can assign the team or team member and press next and we can see a sample of what that looks like. 

We are solving for changing schedules/emergency calls, and are not built out for Delivery companies and is designed for web use not Mobile.

grades.question: Difference between standard and Serialized Inventory tracking?
grades.answer_key: Standard tracks quantities of identical items in bulk. Serialized tracks each individual item with a unique serial number. Serialized has preconfigured Asset templates where serial number auto-fills.

grades.question: Value props of Pricebook? How explain to prospect?
grades.answer_key: Simple tool for technicians to create estimates/invoices quickly using preset flat-rate items and bundles. Build custom estimate/invoice on-site with tiered choices (Good/Better/Best). Present for signature and payment. Makes upselling easier, reduces quoting time, helps close jobs in the field.
grades.student_response:
Pricebook is a flat-rate sales tool that trade businesses can use to supercharge their sales process and increase revenue. Build and deploy your own custom pricebooks with ease, create tiered pricing with our ‘Good, Better, Best’ variant proposal options, and even take signatures and payments. Our pricebook helps turn your technicians into full-fledged salesmen, making upsells and boosting your customer satisfaction easier than ever.

We can create custom pricebooks easily to have good, better, best options to help technicians upsell and boost customer satisfaction.
grades.comment: Missing most key points: tiered choices Good/Better/Best, upselling, reduces quoting time, close in field

grades.question: How is a visual graph/chart created in Reporting?
grades.answer_key: Run report, highlight data, right click or double click, select Chart, make edits. Yes it can be added to Dashboard.
grades.student_response:
create a visual graph or chart inside Reporting, follow these steps:
Navigate to Reporting under the Management tab  
Select a record type (e.g., Jobs) and choose Raw Data Beta  
Customize your date range and click Load Data  
Apply desired filters, groupings, or columns to the report  
Highlight the specific fields to include, right-click, and hover over Chart Range to select a visual report type  
Use the Settings, Data, and Format tabs to customize the chart's appearance, categories, and legend
grades.comment: Hit key points: 3/5

grades.question: How to build a report for technician callbacks?
grades.answer_key: Select month date range > Site Visit report > group by technician.
grades.student_response:
To build a report for technician call backs or service go backs over the last month, follow these steps:
Navigate to the Reporting section and select the ReportingController  
Select the technicianSales report, which is specifically designed to compute performance metrics per technician  
Apply a date range filter for the "last month" to capture the relevant period  
Ensure the report includes the Call Backs metric, which identifies jobs tagged as "callback"  
Alternatively, if you use Site Visit records for "Go Backs," filter your site visit reports by the "Service Go Back" status within your custom workflow  
Review the generated table, which will display the Jobs Count, Call Backs, and Call Back Percentage for each technician

grades.question: What are the different user roles within FieldPulse?
grades.answer_key: Admin, Team Manager, Service Agent, Limited Agent, Assignment User
grades.student_response: Administrator, Team Manager, Service Agent, Limited Agent, Assignment User

grades.question: At a high level, how would you describe how each of these user roles differ?
grades.answer_key: Admin users have access to everything. Team Managers and Service Agents have access to web & mobile app with configurable permissions toggled on/off by admins. Limited Agents can only access mobile app, can only upload files, use timesheets, be assigned to a team, can only see jobs directly assigned to them. Assignment Users have no access to web or mobile app, can be assigned to jobs/tasks/subtasks, receive email when assigned.
key_points: Team Manager/Service Agent = web & mobile with configurable permissions
Limited Agent = mobile only, limited visibility
Assignment User = no app access, email notifications only
grades.student_response:
Administrators have full access to the FieldPulse account. This role is best assigned to the company owner and the office manager. 
Team Managers can create and assign work to their team members. Each Team Manager can be assigned to lead multiple teams. They do not have the ability to view Company Settings or other teams' schedules. 
The Service Agent role is designed for technicians out in the field. Their permission settings can be adjusted as desired. 
The Limited Agent role is designed to see the work they are assigned via the mobile app only. This user can be assigned to a team. 
This user is designed to receive an email outlining the job details. This user does not have access to the FieldPulse app or the ability to use any of its functionality. Contractors are a great example of an Assignment User.

grades.question: What is the difference between Parent-Child relationships and Multi-Location/Multi-Contact?
grades.answer_key: Parent-Child: each account syncs separately to QB, flexible billing (bill to parent OR child). Multi-Location/Multi-Contact: Add multiple service locations and contacts under one OG customer record, only the main OG customer syncs to QB.
key_points: only main customer syncs to QB
grades.student_response:
The Parent/Child structure is a hierarchical system where one main account oversees several smaller accounts.

Parent: The primary account, like a corporate office or property management company. This is usually the main billing entity.

Child: The individual service location, such as a specific condo unit or property site.
grades.comment: Missing most key points: Parent-Child syncs separately to QB, Multi-Location under one customer record, only main customer syncs to QB

grades.question: Do Net Due Dates on Parent automatically apply to Child?
grades.answer_key: The actual values don't transfer, but the 'rule' does. For example, if parent has estimates due in 15 days and invoices due in 90 days, child records will follow those same rules.
key_points: child follows parent's due date rules

grades.section: General Knowledge: Projects, Jobs, Site Visits, & Tasks
grades.question: What is the difference between Projects, Jobs, and Site Visits?
grades.answer_key: Projects: folder that stores/organizes Job & Site Visit records, for long term work. Jobs: standalone or part of a Project, for work typically completed that day (standalone) or over several days/weeks (part of project). Site Visits: subcomponent of a Job record, for callbacks/go backs/return visits.
key_points: Jobs = standalone or part of project
Site Visits = subcomponent of Job for callbacks/return visits
grades.student_response: Projects are the overall blueprint, jobs/work orders are the individual tasks that fall under those and site visits are call backs within the above

grades.question: ClearPath pitch in 4 sentences or less
grades.answer_key: With ClearPath, you, as a business owner, can customize the instructions and actions available to your technicians during each step of their workflow. This ensures that your technicians no longer question what to do or where to go within the mobile app; everything they need will be on one main screen.
grades.student_response: With ClearPath, you, as a business owner, can customize the instructions and actions available to your technicians during each step of their workflow. This ensures that technicians will no longer question what to do or where to go within the app; everything they need will be on one main screen.

grades.question: List at least 3 value props of ClearPath.
grades.answer_key: Consistency for customers because each tech completes job same way; save time because guesswork removed; easier onboarding of new team members; save time with templates for Estimates and Forms; save time with default prepopulated text for comments.
grades.student_response:
Save Time: By having all necessary information in one place, technicians can complete jobs more quickly.

Consistency: By taking the time to set this up, you ensure that every job is completed to your standard of service, regardless of which technician is assigned. This consistency contributes to a more positive experience for your customers.

Easier Training: Your technicians will find it simpler to learn the platform, making onboarding and adoption much smoother.
grades.comment: Got some key points (2/5). Has: consistency, easier onboarding. Missing: save time/remove guesswork, templates, prepopulated text

grades.question: Describe the relationship between Job Custom Status Workflows, Status Action Flows, and Focus View
grades.answer_key: Job Custom Status Workflows are the high-level Statuses common to a type of work. Status Action Flows allow the business to outline the steps and instructions for each specific job they perform (e.g., Shower Installation vs Toilet Installation). Focus View provides the custom mobile experience that neatly displays all of this information for each technician.
key_points: Status Action Flows = specific steps/instructions per job type
grades.student_response: whole workflow, each individual piece of workflow and the focus view is what the individual tech sees in the field.
grades.comment: Missing most key points: Custom Status Workflows = high-level statuses, Status Action Flows = specific steps/instructions per job type, Focus View = custom mobile experience

grades.question: How to build a report for technician callbacks?
grades.answer_key: Select month date range > Site Visit report > group by technician.
grades.student_response:
To build a report for technician call backs or service go backs over the last month, follow these steps:
Navigate to the Reporting section and select the ReportingController
Select the technicianSales report, which is specifically designed to compute performance metrics per technician
Apply a date range filter for the "last month" to capture the relevant period
Ensure the report includes the Call Backs metric, which identifies jobs tagged as "callback"
Alternatively, if you use Site Visit records for "Go Backs," filter your site visit reports by the "Service Go Back" status within your custom workflow
Review the generated table, which will display the Jobs Count, Call Backs, and Call Back Percentage for each technician

grades.question: What is 10DLC compliance?
grades.answer_key: All numbers for SMS to U.S. numbers must be approved through The Campaign Registry (TCR). Industry-wide compliance program enforced by mobile carriers to reduce spam and protect consumers.
grades.student_response: Whether you are purchasing a new number through Engage or porting in an existing number from an outside carrier, all numbers for organizations that send SMS messages to U.S. numbers must get their numbers approved through The Campaign Registry (TCR).

grades.question: What is the two-step 10DLC compliance process?

grades.question: What ensures QB Desktop sync is successful?

grades.student_response: The setup must be completed in single-user mode within QuickBooks Desktop.   We support Contractor, Pro, Premier, and Enterprise editions of QuickBooks Desktop. Settings > Within Window > System > Power and Sleep > Set to ‘Never.’   You must have a stable internet connection.   If you utilize Customer Communications within FieldPulse:  Turn off your Automatic Triggers linked to Estimate and Invoice records.

grades.question: Describe the relationship between Job Custom Status Workflows, Status Action Flows, and Focus View
grades.answer_key: Job Custom Status Workflows are the high-level Statuses common to a type of work. Status Action Flows allow the business to outline the steps and instructions for each specific job they perform (e.g., Shower Installation vs Toilet Installation). Focus View provides the custom mobile experience that neatly displays all of this information for each technician.
key_points: Status Action Flows = specific steps/instructions per job type
grades.student_response:
Job Custom Status Workflows are the high-level blueprints that define the stages a job moves through, such as "New," "On Site," or "Completed"  

Status Action Flows are the specific steps or variants tailored to each status in that blueprint. They determine exactly which instructions and action buttons (like "Text Customer" or "Create Invoice") appear at each stage  

Focus View is the mobile-first interface that displays this information. It acts as a set of "guardrails," showing only the specific widgets, instructions, and buttons the technician needs for their current status to reduce confusion

grades.question: Are Action Buttons, Status Instructions, and Widgets all required at all times?
grades.answer_key: Nope! A customer can choose to just use Action Buttons and/or Status Instructions OR take it further by enabling Focus View and utilizing Widgets.
grades.comment: Got yes/no right but could add more context: can choose which to use, Widgets require Focus View

grades.question: How to build a report for technician callbacks?
grades.answer_key: Select month date range > Site Visit report > group by technician.
grades.student_response:
To build a report for technician call backs or service go backs over the last month, follow these steps:
Navigate to the Reporting section and select the ReportingController
Select the technicianSales report, which is specifically designed to compute performance metrics per technician
Apply a date range filter for the "last month" to capture the relevant period
Ensure the report includes the Call Backs metric, which identifies jobs tagged as "callback"
Alternatively, if you use Site Visit records for "Go Backs," filter your site visit reports by the "Service Go Back" status within your custom workflow
Review the generated table, which will display the Jobs Count, Call Backs, and Call Back Percentage for each technician

ungraded_responses.question: Value props of Custom Forms?
ungraded_responses.response:
Operational Efficiency: Automates the transition from pen-and-paper to digital workflows, reducing the time spent assembling job documentation by an estimated 40%  
Standardization: Ensures consistent data collection through predefined fields and mandatory checklists, which helps support compliance-heavy industries  
Customer Engagement: Enables professional communication via self-service customer intake and feedback forms, improving overall service quality  
Accessibility: Supports offline mode on mobile devices, allowing field technicians to complete documentation without an active internet connection  
Data Integration: Capable of cascading information from related records like jobs, estimates, and invoices to reduce redundant data entry  
Business Intelligence: Provides robust reporting capabilities on form values to help businesses track completion and analyze work-related documents

ungraded_responses.question: What is our Sales Suite?
ungraded_responses.response: Dynamic proposals and pricebook

ungraded_responses.question: Can Sales Suite components be purchased separately?
ungraded_responses.response: No

ungraded_responses.question: Value props of Fleet Tracking?
ungraded_responses.response:
Improved Efficiency and Productivity: Streamlines operations and resource allocation  
Cost Savings: Reduces operational expenses through better monitoring and management  
Enhanced Safety: Includes driver scoring, rewards, and AI DashCam features like distracted driver detection and real-time audio feedback  
Real-Time Visibility: Allows users to display vehicle locations directly on the FieldPulse Schedule Map View  
Maintenance and Health Monitoring: Includes engine diagnostics and a maintenance module to track vehicle health

grades.question: What are the different user roles within FieldPulse?
grades.answer_key: Admin, Team Manager, Service Agent, Limited Agent, Assignment User
grades.student_response: admin, team manager, service agent, limited agent, assignment User

grades.question: At a high level, how would you describe how each of these user roles differ?
grades.answer_key: Admin users have access to everything. Team Managers and Service Agents have access to web & mobile app with configurable permissions toggled on/off by admins. Limited Agents can only access mobile app, can only upload files, use timesheets, be assigned to a team, can only see jobs directly assigned to them. Assignment Users have no access to web or mobile app, can be assigned to jobs/tasks/subtasks, receive email when assigned.
key_points: Team Manager/Service Agent = web & mobile with configurable permissions
Limited Agent = mobile only, limited visibility
Assignment User = no app access, email notifications only
grades.student_response:
Admin (Owner or Office Manager
Full access (essentially Team Manager)
Team Manager Owner or Office Manager
Team Manager can have full access in our platform, outside of company settings and other teams' schedules (Admin has that ability)
Service Agent (Full time tech in the field)
A service agent can open customer profiles and has the ability to view the customer list page when permissions are enabled. All of these features are accessible via the sidebar in their platform view, which gives users administrative control over what they can see and do within the platform, including viewing customers' phone numbers and email addresses. On the job site, the Service Agent is able to manage jobs by creating jobs assigned to themselves and opening jobs assigned to other users directly from the customer profile. Additionally, they can create and generate operational reports; view and create maintenance agreements, as well as view other agreements on the customer profile; and manage Sales & Inventory, which involves viewing item lists, creating line items, and checking inventory levels in other hubs. The Service Agent also has full access to the Price Book to create, view, and edit estimates, process payments or issue refunds, and access and manage their own personal timesheets.
Limited Agent
Limited Agents can use the mobile app to access and manage their assigned jobs including adding notes, viewing job details, and uploading files, but their visibility is restricted only to jobs they are directly assigned to. Their schedule view and job visibility cannot be customized.
Assignment User (sub contractor, only contacted on as needed basis)
The user can be assigned to jobs, tasks, and subtasks, and will receive an email notification when assigned to a job (if enabled in Master settings).

grades.section: General Knowledge: Projects, Jobs, Site Visits, & Tasks
grades.question: What is the difference between Projects, Jobs, and Site Visits?
grades.answer_key: Projects: folder that stores/organizes Job & Site Visit records, for long term work. Jobs: standalone or part of a Project, for work typically completed that day (standalone) or over several days/weeks (part of project). Site Visits: subcomponent of a Job record, for callbacks/go backs/return visits.
key_points: Jobs = standalone or part of project
Site Visits = subcomponent of Job for callbacks/return visits
grades.student_response:
Projects group related jobs together
Jobs are stand alone work completed that day
Site visits are used when additional visits are needed within a job

grades.question: ClearPath pitch in 4 sentences or less
grades.answer_key: With ClearPath, you, as a business owner, can customize the instructions and actions available to your technicians during each step of their workflow. This ensures that your technicians no longer question what to do or where to go within the mobile app; everything they need will be on one main screen.
grades.student_response:
Think about the outliers in your business, the tech who consistently sells more, finishes faster, and follows the process exactly how you’d want it done. 
Now imagine if your entire team performed at that level. That’s exactly where we come in.
We give you industry-specific software combined with custom workflows tailored to your business, acting as guardrails to ensure every job is run the right way, every time. 
These workflows guide your team step by step: prompting upsells, offering maintenance plans, and following your ideal sales process, so your techs consistently perform like your best rep.
grades.comment: Hit key points: 3/5

grades.question: List at least 3 value props of ClearPath.
grades.answer_key: Consistency for customers because each tech completes job same way; save time because guesswork removed; easier onboarding of new team members; save time with templates for Estimates and Forms; save time with default prepopulated text for comments.
grades.student_response: prompting upsells, offering maintenance plans, and following your ideal sales process
grades.comment: Missing most key points: consistency, save time/remove guesswork, easier onboarding, templates, prepopulated text

grades.question: Describe the relationship between Job Custom Status Workflows, Status Action Flows, and Focus View
grades.answer_key: Job Custom Status Workflows are the high-level Statuses common to a type of work. Status Action Flows allow the business to outline the steps and instructions for each specific job they perform (e.g., Shower Installation vs Toilet Installation). Focus View provides the custom mobile experience that neatly displays all of this information for each technician.
key_points: Status Action Flows = specific steps/instructions per job type
grades.student_response:
Clearpath has custom job stages (i.e. “scheduled”, “one the way”, or “in progress”) these are called the Custom Status Workflow

Each stage will have steps, or different actions available to the techs while the job is in that stage (i.e. “send on-the-way text to customer”, “upload picture”, or “create invoice”) These are called the Status Action Flows

The way these different actions and other widgets are presented to the field tech on the mobile app is called Focus View
grades.comment: Got some key points (1/3). Has: Focus View = custom mobile experience. Missing: Custom Status Workflows = high-level statuses, Status Action Flows = specific steps/instructions per job type

grades.question: Are Action Buttons, Status Instructions, and Widgets all required at all times?
grades.answer_key: Nope! A customer can choose to just use Action Buttons and/or Status Instructions OR take it further by enabling Focus View and utilizing Widgets.
grades.comment: Got yes/no right but could add more context: can choose which to use, Widgets require Focus View

grades.question: How can Material Lists be used? Give two examples.

key_points: Pre Estimate step for billing items
grades.student_response:
When you visit a job site, you can make a list of all the materials you’ll need to get the work done. This helps you know exactly what to buy. Or an alternative to this is your company creating an estimate first. The technician can then fill out the material list based on the materials needed to complete the work or for what they actually used during the job.
After you create the list, a colleague can check it. This list can then be used to create an estimate (a rough cost of the job) or an invoice (the final bill).
You can choose to include some or all of the materials from your list in the estimate or invoice so the customer knows exactly what they’re paying for.
grades.comment: Missing most key points: Go List/Checklist for loading truck, Pre Estimate step for billing items, two distinct examples

grades.question: What limitations of child record estimate/invoice workflow cause problems?
grades.answer_key: The invoice remains tied to the child record, so you don't get true billing from parent to child. For proper parent-level billing the invoice must be created under the parent account.
key_points: invoice must be created under parent
grades.student_response: For many commercial and property-management customers, the simple "send to parent" function is insufficient because they require a system that can reliably manage specific billing needs: correctly identifying who receives the estimate or invoice (the dedicated billing or AP contact) beyond just listing where it's billed (the billing address); supporting different billing recipients for each property or location; and ensuring consistent routing of billing communications without depending on internal staff tribal knowledge.
grades.comment: Got some key points (1/3). Has: no true parent-to-child billing. Missing: invoice tied to child record, invoice must be created under parent

grades.question: Difference between standard and Serialized Inventory tracking?
grades.answer_key: Standard tracks quantities of identical items in bulk. Serialized tracks each individual item with a unique serial number. Serialized has preconfigured Asset templates where serial number auto-fills.
grades.student_response: Standard Inventory for high-volume consumables, tracking only total quantity and deducting stock automatically upon invoicing. For high-value or warranty-sensitive gear, Serialized Inventory provides unit-level traceability by requiring you to log and select unique serial numbers for each item sold. While Standard is best for low-risk stock, Serialized ensures precise tracking by removing specific units from your records only after a serial number is selected and payment is processed.
grades.comment: Missing key points: standard = bulk quantities, serialized has asset templates

grades.question: Can Custom Fields be added to Inventory Line Items? Give examples.
grades.answer_key: Yes! Examples: Aisle, Shelf, Bin Number.
grades.student_response: Yes. You can organize your stock by specifying the exact Aisle, Shelf, and Bin where each item is stored for precise location tracking.
grades.comment: Partial answer. Has: Yes. Missing: examples like Aisle/Shelf/Bin Number

grades.question: When does Inventory get deducted?
grades.answer_key: Users select deduction preference: Material List (at Completed status) or Invoice (at Invoiced/Finalized status).
grades.student_response: When a Material List is Completed or an Invoice is Invoiced/Finalized

grades.question: Can a user decide which hub Inventory deducts from?
grades.answer_key: No, Inventory deducted from the Material List or Invoice creator's assigned hub every time.
grades.comment: Got yes/no right but could add more context: creator's assigned hub

grades.question: Can FieldPulse support truck-swapping technicians for Inventory?
grades.answer_key: No, doesn't track cross-truck usage in real time. Would be a huge manual lift, should not recommend.
grades.comment: Got yes/no right but could add more context: no real-time cross-truck tracking, huge manual lift

grades.question: How can supplier/vendor website links be saved for quick access?
grades.answer_key: Via Item List, add URL to Item Link section for each Line Item (Mobile) or click Add Link button next to Unit Cost (Web).
grades.student_response: You can save and access supplier information, including website links, through the Supplier Chat (powered by ProKeep) and Supplier Integrations features in FieldPulse
grades.comment: Missing key points: Item List, Item Link section, Add Link button

grades.question: Can FieldPulse validate inventory availability in real time?
grades.answer_key: No. Inventory levels are not checked in real time when adding items to estimates, invoices, or material lists.
grades.comment: Got yes/no right but could add more context: not checked in real time

grades.question: Best practice for reallocating materials after bulk shipment?
grades.answer_key: Manually move materials to trucks. Cannot have POs send inventory to multiple warehouses or trucks. Mobile: adjust quantities with +/- buttons per Hub. Web: similar manual adjustment.
grades.student_response: Our best practice recommendation is to receive everything into a central hub first, then manually reallocate counts to each truck hub. In the mobile app, you can manually adjust quantities. In the web app you can scan everything in and easily transfer within half
grades.comment: Missing most key points: manual process, cannot auto-distribute from POs, mobile +/- buttons, web adjustment

grades.question: How many suppliers/vendors on one PO?
grades.answer_key: Only one.
grades.student_response: On a single Purchase Order record in FieldPulse, you can list only one supplier/vendor.
grades.comment: Partial answer. Has: one. Missing: 1

grades.question: Does FieldPulse support partial receiving on a PO?
grades.answer_key: No!

grades.question: What is Supplier Chat? Value props?
grades.answer_key: Direct access to 8,500+ suppliers across 25 industries. Free feature. Can ask about inventory, pricing, order status, how to place an order. Can create PO and send via Supplier Chat. Available on web AND mobile.
grades.student_response: Supplier Chat is a built-in messaging tool in FieldPulse that provides real-time, direct access to over 8,500 suppliers across the U.S.
grades.comment: Missing most key points: 8500+ suppliers, 25 industries, free feature, inventory/pricing/orders, web and mobile, send POs

grades.question: What Commission structures are available?
grades.answer_key: Company Default Commission, User-Specific Commission, Flat Rate Job Commissions (Pricebook), Ad-Hoc Commission.
grades.student_response: FieldPulse tracks commissions through both system-generated and manual entries, ranging from Percentage of Gross Sales where payments are automatically calculated based on the invoice total to Flat-Rate amounts for specific jobs or upsells. Managers can also issue Spiffs or Bonuses, which are event-based incentives such as rewards for receiving five-star customer reviews. Ultimately, the system distinguishes between System Generated commissions, which follow predefined programs like membership sales, and Manual entries, which allow admins to key in ad-hoc, one-off bonuses or fixed dollar amounts.
grades.comment: Got the gist (2/4). Missing: Company Default, User-Specific

grades.question: Can Commission be split?
grades.answer_key: Yes, but at the moment this is a manual process, not automatic.
grades.comment: Expected: yes. Student answered: no

grades.question: Can FieldPulse run payroll or integrate with payroll software?
grades.answer_key: No. Can bring in time but that's it. Export time and use outside of platform.
grades.comment: Got yes/no right but could add more context: can export time

grades.question: Value props of Pricebook? How explain to prospect?
grades.answer_key: Simple tool for technicians to create estimates/invoices quickly using preset flat-rate items and bundles. Build custom estimate/invoice on-site with tiered choices (Good/Better/Best). Present for signature and payment. Makes upselling easier, reduces quoting time, helps close jobs in the field.
grades.student_response:
Pricebook is your ‘menu’ of services inside FieldPulse. Instead of building estimates line-by-line, your tech taps the job they’re doing—then FieldPulse auto-builds the estimate with your pricing, so every tech quotes the same way. It also makes upsells feel natural, because you can show Good/Better/Best options side-by-side. The customer chooses what they want, you collect a signature/payment, and you convert it straight into an estimate or invoice in a couple clicks.
Pricebook turns your service offerings into a sellable, profitable menu—so quoting is fast, pricing is consistent, and techs can easily present options.
grades.comment: Missing most key points: flat-rate items, tiered choices Good/Better/Best, upselling, reduces quoting time

grades.question: What does flat rate pricing mean?
grades.answer_key: One set price for a job or service instead of billing by hour or by each part. Customer knows total cost upfront.
grades.student_response: Flat Rate Pricing is a "value-based" billing method where you charge customers a single, fixed price for a specific service—such as $250 for a Water Heater Tune-Up—regardless of how long the job actually takes.
grades.comment: Missing key points: not hourly, customer knows cost upfront

grades.question: Can Price Tiers be managed through Pricebook?
grades.answer_key: No, Price Tiers are tied to Customer records not Pricebook.
grades.comment: Got yes/no right but could add more context: tied to Customer records

grades.question: Can each Pricebook have different permissions per user?
grades.answer_key: No! Permissions cannot be set per pricebook. Access control applies to the Pricebook feature as a whole.
grades.comment: Expected: no. Student answered: yes

grades.question: What industries utilize Pricebook the most?
grades.answer_key: HVAC, Plumbing, Garage Door
grades.comment: Got the gist (2/3). Missing: Garage Door

grades.question: Main differences between Wisetack and Acorn Financing?
grades.answer_key: Wisetack: 3.9% convenience fee, $500-$25,000 loan range, FieldPulse user must apply (US based, 1 year in business, $150K revenue, 10+ reviews at 4.5+ stars). Acorn: No application for FieldPulse user, end customer applies directly, Acorn wires funds to end customer, $1,000-$100,000 loan range.
key_points: Acorn: no application, end customer applies, $1K-100K, funds to end customer
grades.student_response: Acorn has a $0 cost to the contractor while wisetack has a 3.9%, Also Acorn has a loan amount of 1,000 - 100,000 while wisetack has a lone amount of 500 - 25,000

The reason why that's important is because other FSM/Crm charge anywhere between 8%-!0% of the contractor's job.

grades.student_response: Fees shift to 3.5% for next business days for credit cards and 1.5% for ACH

grades.question: What is Card Fee Recovery (CFR)?
grades.answer_key: A compliant dual pricing program that allows customers to pass on card processing fees to their customers.
grades.student_response: Card Fee Recovery is FieldPulse’s built-in way to help merchants recoup credit card processing costs in a transparent, compliant way without using a traditional surcharge.

grades.question: What does CFR look like in FieldPulse?
grades.answer_key: CFR dynamically increases line item price by chosen %. Invoice displays higher Total Card Price (with fees) and Total ACH/Cash Price (base price, since illegal to pass on ACH fees).
key_points: illegal to pass on ACH fees
grades.student_response: In FieldPulse, Card Fee Recovery mainly changes how invoices/estimates and payment screens look so the customer clearly sees a card price vs a cash/ACH price
grades.comment: Some overlap but missing major points: dynamically increases line item price, Total ACH/Cash Price, illegal to pass on ACH fees

grades.question: Can customer choose what % of fees to pass on with CFR?
grades.answer_key: Yes! Recommends 3%, but can pass on whatever they want.
grades.comment: Got yes/no right but could add more context: recommends 3%, flexible

grades.question: Fees with Card Fee Recovery?
grades.answer_key: 4.0% & 1.0% ACH (ACH fees cannot be passed on)
grades.student_response: For standard funding (Rainforest), the default fee distribution is 1% to the FieldPulse customer and 3% to the end customer. If Next-Day Funding is utilized, the default distribution changes to 1.5% for the FieldPulse customer and 3% for the end customer.
grades.comment: Missing key points: 4%, 1% ACH

grades.question: Fees with Next-Business Day Funding AND CFR?
grades.answer_key: 4.5% & 1.5% ACH (ACH fees cannot be passed on)
grades.student_response: Next-Day Funding is utilized, the default distribution changes to 1.5% for the FieldPulse customer and 3% for the end customer.
grades.comment: Partial answer. Has: 1.5% ACH. Missing: 4.5%

grades.question: Why is Reporting important?

grades.student_response: This feature allows the customer to pull reports from important aspects of the business. The reason why it is important is because now our customers have the ability to fully see the reports from the raw data available within FieldPulse. This is important because now our customers have the ability to find inefficiencies within their business if there is any and also can see if there's any gaps within estimates, Jobs, payments, timesheets, ECT.
grades.comment: Missing most key points: decision-making, performance tracking, customer service, operational efficiency, financial management

grades.question: What user roles have access to Reporting?

grades.question: How is a visual graph/chart created in Reporting?
grades.answer_key: Run report, highlight data, right click or double click, select Chart, make edits. Yes it can be added to Dashboard.
grades.student_response: To create visual charts, you must first generate a Raw Data report by navigating to Management → Reporting, picking a record type, choosing Raw Data (or Raw Data Beta), setting your date type + date range, and clicking Load Data. Once the data table is loaded, you can customize it using Filters, Groupings, and Columns. Next, highlight the cells/columns you want to visualize in the table, right-click on the highlighted area, hover over “Chart Range”, and select your visual report type. Finally, fine-tune the chart in the chart panel using the three tabs: Settings (chart type, color scheme), Data (categories/series inclusion/exclusion), and Format (fonts, legend, and other visual details). Yes it can be added to the dashboard as a widget.

grades.question: Can you create custom dashboards for different user roles?
