grades.question: Can all user roles access Offline Mode?
grades.answer_key: No! Only Admin, Team Managers, and Service Agents.
grades.comment: Got yes/no right but could add more context: Admin, Team Managers, Service Agents only

grades.question: What can be done in Offline Mode?
grades.answer_key: Whole app is NOT accessible. Customers: cache related customers, access/edit 200 existing Custom Forms, create unlimited new draft forms. Jobs: cache 1 week, change status, upload 50 photos, save notes, access/edit 200 forms. Projects: cache 1 week, access/edit 200 forms. Custom Forms: up to 20 templates. Estimates & Invoices: cache 600 total, search 10K line items, view cached payments, preview finalized records, gather signatures, update status, 100 estimate/100 invoice templates. Cannot: collect payments, generate PDF. New drafts: can create linked to records, cannot preview/convert/collect signatures/payments/PDF.
grades.student_response: Offline, users can cache customers, and their related Jobs and Projects (Job/Project data caches for one week). For cached records (Jobs, Projects, and Customers), users can access/edit up to 200 existing Custom Forms each (600 total) and create unlimited new draft forms, plus access up to 20 Custom Form templates. Job activities supported offline include uploading up to 50 photos per Job, saving notes, and changing status. For Estimates and Invoices, up to 600 total cached records can be accessed, utilizing a cached Item List (up to 10,000 items), viewing payment records (read-only), previewing finalized records, collecting signatures (PDFs generated online), updating status, and accessing up to 100 Estimate and 100 Invoice templates. Users can also create new Estimate or Invoice drafts while offline, tied to related records. No, the entire application is not available.
grades.comment: Hit key points: 5/8

grades.question: What is 10DLC compliance?
grades.answer_key: All numbers for SMS to U.S. numbers must be approved through The Campaign Registry (TCR). Industry-wide compliance program enforced by mobile carriers to reduce spam and protect consumers.
grades.student_response: 10DLC compliance means texts from your business will actually be delivered by carriers, because your texting program is correctly registered and your website includes the necessary SMS compliance pages.
grades.comment: Missing most key points: Campaign Registry TCR, industry-wide, mobile carriers, reduce spam, protect consumers

grades.question: What is the two-step 10DLC compliance process?
grades.answer_key: 1) Brand Registration: submitting business details. 2) Campaign Registration: publishing SMS compliance pages on user's website (Facebook groups not accepted).
grades.student_response: following the carrier rules for business texting from standard 10‑digit phone numbers in the U.S. first You register your business/brand identity so carriers know who is texting. Once Brand is approved, you register what you’re texting and prove compliance with carrier rules.
grades.comment: Matched 1/3. Missing: Campaign Registration, compliance pages on website

grades.question: What ensures QB Desktop sync is successful?
grades.answer_key: Sleep mode must be disabled, stable internet connection, disable notifications & communication triggers.
grades.student_response: To ensure successful integration, use a supported QuickBooks Desktop version and run FieldPulse’s Web Connector on a workstation that stays on, in Single-User Mode for initial setup. You must disable sleep mode and maintain a stable internet connection for continuous operation. The QuickBooks user employed by the connector needs all required permissions for Customer, AR, Lists, and related functions. Finally, decide and configure whether you require two-way synchronization or one-way synchronization.

grades.question: What data syncs from QB Online? One way or two way?

grades.question: What info is generally needed for a data migration?

We can’t migrate maintenance agreements or most images/files today.

ungraded_responses.question: What reminders and notifications can we provide?
ungraded_responses.response: The FieldPulse platform utilizes several main communication channels, including Email for job and appointment confirmations/reminders, estimate and invoice send/reminder emails, Assignment User job assignment notifications, and payment events like chargeback alerts. SMS is used for appointment reminders and some booking confirmations and customer updates when enabled. In-app & push notifications deliver user reminders, task follow-ups, and job assignment/updates when a user logs into the mobile or web application. Finally, Fleet-specific alerts, such as geofence and maintenance alerts, are delivered via email, SMS, or in-app notifications through the dedicated fleet system.

grades.section: General Knowledge: Projects, Jobs, Site Visits, & Tasks
grades.question: What is the difference between Projects, Jobs, and Site Visits?
grades.answer_key: Projects: folder that stores/organizes Job & Site Visit records, for long term work. Jobs: standalone or part of a Project, for work typically completed that day (standalone) or over several days/weeks (part of project). Site Visits: subcomponent of a Job record, for callbacks/go backs/return visits.
key_points: Jobs = standalone or part of project
Site Visits = subcomponent of Job for callbacks/return visits
grades.student_response:
Projects group related jobs together
Jobs are stand alone work completed that day
Site visits are used when additional visits are needed within a job

grades.question: ClearPath pitch in 4 sentences or less
grades.answer_key: With ClearPath, you, as a business owner, can customize the instructions and actions available to your technicians during each step of their workflow. This ensures that your technicians no longer question what to do or where to go within the mobile app; everything they need will be on one main screen.
grades.student_response:
Think about the outliers in your business, the tech who consistently sells more, finishes faster, and follows the process exactly how you’d want it done. 
Now imagine if your entire team performed at that level. That’s exactly where we come in.
We give you industry-specific software combined with custom workflows tailored to your business, acting as guardrails to ensure every job is run the right way, every time. 
These workflows guide your team step by step: prompting upsells, offering maintenance plans, and following your ideal sales process, so your techs consistently perform like your best rep.
grades.comment: Hit key points: 3/5

grades.question: List at least 3 value props of ClearPath.
grades.answer_key: Consistency for customers because each tech completes job same way; save time because guesswork removed; easier onboarding of new team members; save time with templates for Estimates and Forms; save time with default prepopulated text for comments.
grades.student_response:
Increasing average order size
Boosting conversion rates
And doing it all with far less oversight
grades.comment: Missing most key points: consistency, save time/remove guesswork, easier onboarding, templates, prepopulated text

grades.question: Describe the relationship between Job Custom Status Workflows, Status Action Flows, and Focus View
grades.answer_key: Job Custom Status Workflows are the high-level Statuses common to a type of work. Status Action Flows allow the business to outline the steps and instructions for each specific job they perform (e.g., Shower Installation vs Toilet Installation). Focus View provides the custom mobile experience that neatly displays all of this information for each technician.
key_points: Status Action Flows = specific steps/instructions per job type
grades.student_response:
Clearpath has custom job stages (i.e. “scheduled”, “one the way”, or “in progress”) these are called the Custom Status Workflow

Each stage will have steps, or different actions available to the techs while the job is in that stage (i.e. “send on-the-way text to customer”, “upload picture”, or “create invoice”) These are called the Status Action Flows

The way these different actions and other widgets are presented to the field tech on the mobile app is called Focus View
grades.comment: Got some key points (1/3). Has: Focus View = custom mobile experience. Missing: Custom Status Workflows = high-level statuses, Status Action Flows = specific steps/instructions per job type

grades.question: How can Material Lists be used? Give two examples.

grades.student_response:
When you visit a job site, you can make a list of all the materials you’ll need to get the work done. This helps you know exactly what to buy. Or an alternative to this is your company creating an estimate first. The technician can then fill out the material list based on the materials needed to complete the work or for what they actually used during the job.
After you create the list, a colleague can check it. This list can then be used to create an estimate (a rough cost of the job) or an invoice (the final bill).
You can choose to include some or all of the materials from your list in the estimate or invoice so the customer knows exactly what they’re paying for.

grades.question: What limitations of child record estimate/invoice workflow cause problems?

key_points: invoice must be created under parent
grades.student_response: For many commercial and property-management customers, the simple "send to parent" function is insufficient because they require a system that can reliably manage specific billing needs: correctly identifying who receives the estimate or invoice (the dedicated billing or AP contact) beyond just listing where it's billed (the billing address); supporting different billing recipients for each property or location; and ensuring consistent routing of billing communications without depending on internal staff tribal knowledge.

grades.question: Difference between standard and Serialized Inventory tracking?
grades.answer_key: Standard tracks quantities of identical items in bulk. Serialized tracks each individual item with a unique serial number. Serialized has preconfigured Asset templates where serial number auto-fills.
grades.student_response: Standard Inventory for high-volume consumables, tracking only total quantity and deducting stock automatically upon invoicing. For high-value or warranty-sensitive gear, Serialized Inventory provides unit-level traceability by requiring you to log and select unique serial numbers for each item sold. While Standard is best for low-risk stock, Serialized ensures precise tracking by removing specific units from your records only after a serial number is selected and payment is processed.
grades.comment: Missing key points: standard = bulk quantities, serialized has asset templates

grades.question: What can be done in Offline Mode?
grades.answer_key: Whole app is NOT accessible. Customers: cache related customers, access/edit 200 existing Custom Forms, create unlimited new draft forms. Jobs: cache 1 week, change status, upload 50 photos, save notes, access/edit 200 forms. Projects: cache 1 week, access/edit 200 forms. Custom Forms: up to 20 templates. Estimates & Invoices: cache 600 total, search 10K line items, view cached payments, preview finalized records, gather signatures, update status, 100 estimate/100 invoice templates. Cannot: collect payments, generate PDF. New drafts: can create linked to records, cannot preview/convert/collect signatures/payments/PDF.
grades.student_response: Offline, users can cache customers, and their related Jobs and Projects (Job/Project data caches for one week). For cached records (Jobs, Projects, and Customers), users can access/edit up to 200 existing Custom Forms each (600 total) and create unlimited new draft forms, plus access up to 20 Custom Form templates. Job activities supported offline include uploading up to 50 photos per Job, saving notes, and changing status. For Estimates and Invoices, up to 600 total cached records can be accessed, utilizing a cached Item List (up to 10,000 items), viewing payment records (read-only), previewing finalized records, collecting signatures (PDFs generated online), updating status, and accessing up to 100 Estimate and 100 Invoice templates. Users can also create new Estimate or Invoice drafts while offline, tied to related records. No, the entire application is not available.
grades.comment: Hit key points: 5/8

grades.question: How do Job records sync with QB?
grades.answer_key: They do NOT sync.
grades.student_response:
Job record syncing differs between QuickBooks Online (QBO) and QuickBooks Desktop (QBD).

QuickBooks Online (QBO)

Data primarily syncs via Invoices and Customers in a two-way integration. FieldPulse job invoices sync to QBO, including customer and line item details. Conversely, QBO invoices appear in FieldPulse for job management. Historical data can be synced entirely or from a specific date upon onboarding.

QuickBooks Desktop (QBD)

QBD syncing focuses on operational records, including some one-way flows:
Records: Customers, estimates, and invoices sync bidirectionally.
Timesheets: Sync one-way from FieldPulse to QuickBooks Time (clock-in/out and duration).
Purchase Orders: Sync one-way from FieldPulse to QBD (once enabled).
Sub-customers: Only one parent/child level syncs to FieldPulse.

grades.question: Can QB Projects sync into FP?
grades.answer_key: Yes, but not recommended because they sync as Related Customers.
grades.comment: Missing key points: Yes but not recommended, sync as Related Customers

grades.question: What data syncs from QB Online? One way or two way?
grades.answer_key: Customers (two way), Line Items (two way), Estimates (two way), Invoices (two way), Payments (two way), Purchase Orders (two way), Vendors (two way)
grades.student_response:
FieldPulse offers two-way sync for most data: Customers, Invoices (including line items, discounts, taxes), Estimates, Payments (date, method, amount), and Invoice Items/Products & Services.


One-way sync occurs for:
Purchase Orders: FieldPulse to QuickBooks Online only.
Projects: QuickBooks Online to FieldPulse (as related customers); FieldPulse-created projects do not sync back.

grades.question: What data syncs from QB Desktop? One way or two way?
grades.answer_key: Customers (two way), Line Items (two way), Estimates (two way), Invoices (two way), Payments (two way), Purchase Orders (ONE WAY - FP to QB)
key_points: Purchase Orders ONE WAY FP to QB
grades.student_response:
FieldPulse offers two-way sync for most data: Customers, Invoices (including line items, discounts, taxes), Estimates, Payments (date, method, amount), and Invoice Items/Products & Services.


One-way sync occurs for:
Purchase Orders: FieldPulse to QuickBooks Online only.
Projects: QuickBooks Online to FieldPulse (as related customers); FieldPulse-created projects do not sync back.




The QuickBooks Desktop (QBD) integration primarily uses a two-way sync, though a FieldPulse-to-QBD one-way sync is available on request.


Two-Way Sync Data: Customers (including notes/status), Line Items, Estimates, Invoices (matching QBD numbering), and Payments sync both ways.


One-Way Sync Data: Timesheets and Purchase Orders sync from FieldPulse to QBD. Vendors, Classes, and Tax Codes sync from QBD to FieldPulse.


Sync Limitations: Syncs fail if Customer/Item names exceed 31 characters or contain special characters ($ & # @ + { } :). Unsupported items include Subclasses, multiple tax codes, negative line items, credit memos, and sales receipts.

grades.question: What is a Business Class in QB?

grades.question: What is a Sales Account in QB?
grades.answer_key: Income account that records revenue from products and services, appears on Profit & Loss report.

grades.question: When does an Estimate/Invoice sync to QB?
grades.answer_key: Estimate syncs when in Sent type of Status. Invoice syncs when in Invoiced type of Status.
grades.student_response: An Estimate or Invoice record syncs to QuickBooks in the following scenarios: Invoices: Syncing occurs immediately when a field technician converts an estimate to an invoice, creates a new invoice for a completed job   1 , marks an invoice as 'Invoiced', or applies a payment to it   2 . Estimates: These are synced to QuickBooks for accounting purposes when created in the field   1 . Updates: Any editing or updating of an existing invoice or payment record will automatically trigger a sync to update the information in QuickBooks   2 . Two-Way Sync: If an invoice is created directly in QuickBooks, a webhook notifies the system to create or update the corresponding record in FieldPulse
grades.comment: Partial answer. Has: Invoice at Invoiced status. Missing: Estimate at Sent status

grades.question: Does Inventory sync from both QB Online and Desktop?
grades.answer_key: Yes.

grades.question: How does Inventory Quantity sync?
grades.answer_key: Total inventory sync, specific inventory hubs do not.
grades.student_response: Inventory quantity syncs between FieldPulse and QuickBooks Online (QBO) based on the following rules: Sync Direction New Items: Sync both ways. Items created in QBO sync to FieldPulse (total quantities go to Hub 1), and items created in FieldPulse sync total inventory to QBO's Qty On Hand   1 . Item Updates: Sync one way only. Changes made in FieldPulse update QBO, but updates made in QBO do not sync back to FieldPulse

grades.question: Does Inventory sync differently if hubs are enabled?
grades.answer_key: No. Updates to item quantities in QBO will not reflect in FP and vice versa. Manage separately.
grades.comment: Expected: no. Student answered: yes

ungraded_responses.question: What reminders and notifications can we provide?
ungraded_responses.response: The FieldPulse platform utilizes several main communication channels, including Email for job and appointment confirmations/reminders, estimate and invoice send/reminder emails, Assignment User job assignment notifications, and payment events like chargeback alerts. SMS is used for appointment reminders and some booking confirmations and customer updates when enabled. In-app & push notifications deliver user reminders, task follow-ups, and job assignment/updates when a user logs into the mobile or web application. Finally, Fleet-specific alerts, such as geofence and maintenance alerts, are delivered via email, SMS, or in-app notifications through the dedicated fleet system.

grades.question: What are the different user roles within FieldPulse?

grades.student_response: Admin, Team Manager, Service Agent, Limited Agent, Assignment User

grades.question: At a high level, how would you describe how each of these user roles differ?
grades.answer_key: Admin users have access to everything. Team Managers and Service Agents have access to web & mobile app with configurable permissions toggled on/off by admins. Limited Agents can only access mobile app, can only upload files, use timesheets, be assigned to a team, can only see jobs directly assigned to them. Assignment Users have no access to web or mobile app, can be assigned to jobs/tasks/subtasks, receive email when assigned.
key_points: Team Manager/Service Agent = web & mobile with configurable permissions
Limited Agent = mobile only, limited visibility
Assignment User = no app access, email notifications only
grades.student_response: Admin has total access. Team Manager can have full access in our platform, outside of company settings and other teams' schedules (Admin has that ability). Service Agents (techs in the field) are able to manage jobs by creating jobs assigned to themselves and opening jobs assigned to other users directly from the customer profile. They can also edit, create, and view estimates while in the field. Limited agents are only able to view and work within the specific job that they are assigned, their job visibility and schedule view can't be customized. Assignment User can't access the FP app, but can view job details given through email.

grades.section: General Knowledge: Projects, Jobs, Site Visits, & Tasks
grades.question: What is the difference between Projects, Jobs, and Site Visits?
grades.answer_key: Projects: folder that stores/organizes Job & Site Visit records, for long term work. Jobs: standalone or part of a Project, for work typically completed that day (standalone) or over several days/weeks (part of project). Site Visits: subcomponent of a Job record, for callbacks/go backs/return visits.
key_points: Jobs = standalone or part of project
Site Visits = subcomponent of Job for callbacks/return visits
grades.student_response:
Projects group related jobs together
Jobs are stand alone work completed that day
Site visits are used when additional visits are needed within a job

grades.question: ClearPath pitch in 4 sentences or less
grades.answer_key: With ClearPath, you, as a business owner, can customize the instructions and actions available to your technicians during each step of their workflow. This ensures that your technicians no longer question what to do or where to go within the mobile app; everything they need will be on one main screen.
grades.student_response:
Think about the outliers in your business, the tech who consistently sells more, finishes faster, and follows the process exactly how you’d want it done. 
Now imagine if your entire team performed at that level; that’s exactly where we come in.We give you industry-specific software combined with custom workflows tailored to your business, acting as guardrails to ensure every job is run the right way, every time. These workflows guide your team step by step: prompting upsells, offering maintenance plans, and following your ideal sales process, so your techs consistently perform like your best rep.
grades.comment: Hit key points: 3/5

grades.question: List at least 3 value props of ClearPath.
grades.answer_key: Consistency for customers because each tech completes job same way; save time because guesswork removed; easier onboarding of new team members; save time with templates for Estimates and Forms; save time with default prepopulated text for comments.
grades.student_response:
Increasing average order size 
Boosting conversion rates 
Doing it all with far less oversight
grades.comment: Missing most key points: consistency, save time/remove guesswork, easier onboarding, templates, prepopulated text

grades.question: Describe the relationship between Job Custom Status Workflows, Status Action Flows, and Focus View
grades.answer_key: Job Custom Status Workflows are the high-level Statuses common to a type of work. Status Action Flows allow the business to outline the steps and instructions for each specific job they perform (e.g., Shower Installation vs Toilet Installation). Focus View provides the custom mobile experience that neatly displays all of this information for each technician.
key_points: Status Action Flows = specific steps/instructions per job type
grades.student_response:
Clearpath has custom job stages (i.e. “scheduled”, “one the way”, or “in progress”) these are called the Custom Status Workflow

Each stage will have steps, or different actions available to the techs while the job is in that stage (i.e. “send on-the-way text to customer”, “upload picture”, or “create invoice”) These are called the Status Action Flows

The way these different actions and other widgets are presented to the field tech on the mobile app is called Focus View
grades.comment: Got some key points (1/3). Has: Focus View = custom mobile experience. Missing: Custom Status Workflows = high-level statuses, Status Action Flows = specific steps/instructions per job type

grades.question: Are Action Buttons, Status Instructions, and Widgets all required at all times?
grades.answer_key: Nope! A customer can choose to just use Action Buttons and/or Status Instructions OR take it further by enabling Focus View and utilizing Widgets.
grades.comment: Got yes/no right but could add more context: can choose which to use, Widgets require Focus View

grades.question: Are technicians always locked into Focus View?
grades.answer_key: You can decide on a per Status basis when you want technicians to be locked into Focus View.
grades.student_response: No, technicians are not always locked into Focus View—and yes, you can lock them in for some statuses and not others.
grades.comment: Partial answer. Has: No. Missing: per Status basis

grades.question: What can we create Custom Status Workflows for?
grades.answer_key: Job, Projects, Site Visits, Material Lists, Custom Forms, Estimates, and Invoices
grades.student_response: Jobs, job visits, projects, estimates, invoices, custom forms, material list

grades.question: What are the benefits of some Status types in Custom Status Workflows?
grades.answer_key: Travel Time and In Progress status types track/log time. Pending status type pauses the time tracking.
grades.student_response: Being able to see the flow of work across all the users and allowing you to clearly communicate the progress of a job to the customer
grades.comment: Missing key points: Travel Time tracks time, Pending pauses time tracking

grades.question: What two Status Types are required to save a Custom Status Workflow?
grades.answer_key: A 'New' and 'Completed' type of status are required.

grades.question: What are the 4 schedule views in FieldPulse?
grades.answer_key: List, Calendar, Dispatch, & Map view

grades.question: Can the Schedule be printed?
grades.answer_key: Yes.

grades.question: What does the Hourglass button on the Schedule do?
grades.answer_key: View all unscheduled and unassigned Jobs.

grades.question: How can someone view upcoming Jobs associated with a Maintenance Agreement?
grades.answer_key: Click Hourglass button, then in slide out window select Filter button, then in 'Is a Maintenance Agreement Job' field select 'Yes'.
grades.student_response: Go to agreements and press this month, next month and all
grades.comment: Missing most key points: Hourglass button, Filter button, Yes

grades.question: What does the '...' near the Hourglass button allow customers to do?
grades.answer_key: Adjust the color coding on the Schedule. Color coding can either mirror the Statuses of each Job OR each team member.
grades.comment: Missing key points: color coding, team member

grades.question: How does Route Optimization work? What are the limitations?
grades.answer_key: Schedule a Route or Reassign Jobs from a map view. Limitation is that this process is manual and not automated.
grades.student_response: We do not have Route Optimization
grades.comment: Missing key points: Schedule Route or Reassign Jobs, map view, manual, not automated

grades.question: How do I get access to the Booking Portal?
grades.answer_key: Must be enabled from Features & Plugins page. Then configure via Company Settings > Booking Portal tab.
grades.student_response: Through company settings, then you press features and plug ins then under booking portal then you can open the booking portal settings

grades.question: Do Booking Portal requests auto-create a new Customer record?
grades.answer_key: Yes.

grades.question: Can end customers attach pictures and videos to Booking Portal requests?
grades.answer_key: Yes, they can attach files, photos, and videos, but only if the FieldPulse user configured their Booking Portal that way.
grades.comment: Got yes/no right but could add more context: if configured by FieldPulse user

grades.question: How do I embed the Booking Portal on my website?
grades.answer_key: Embed the iFrame for your Booking Portal URL found in Company Settings > Booking Portal tab.
grades.student_response: Add a book now button that links to the portal
grades.comment: Missing key points: iFrame, Booking Portal URL, Company Settings

grades.question: What is an Asset in FieldPulse? List 2 examples.
grades.answer_key: Asset records allow users to track information about assets they install and/or service for customers as well as track internal equipment. Examples: Water heater, employee computers/phones.
grades.student_response: In FieldPulse, an Asset is the module you use to track physical equipment and property  either your customer’s equipment or your own company’s gear  and tie it into your jobs and maintenance workflowsIn FieldPulse, the Asset module is used to track and manage physical equipment and property. This includes equipment owned by your customers as well as gear belonging to your own company, allowing you to integrate it with your job and maintenance workflows.

grades.question: Why should we showcase Assets before jumping into Maintenance Agreements?
grades.answer_key: The flow is better on a live demo. On the Asset record we can show how to relate the Asset to other record types like Maintenance Agreements and Jobs.
grades.student_response: Shows the customer the value in what it costs and what you need to use to properly maintain the customer's unit.
grades.comment: Missing key points: better demo flow, show relationship to other records, Maintenance Agreements, Jobs

grades.question: Can a Service Agent scan barcodes on assets?
grades.answer_key: Yes!

grades.question: What is a Maintenance Agreement in FieldPulse?
grades.answer_key: A Maintenance Agreement outlines the times a business will go out and perform maintenance on something they installed. Service frequencies typically quarterly or semi-annual. Contracts can be attached after the record has been created and saved.
grades.student_response: A maintenance agreement is a recurring service agreed upon between you the provider and the customer
grades.comment: Missing most key points: outlines maintenance schedule, service frequencies, contracts attached after creation

grades.question: Best practice for MA occurrence and billing creation?
grades.answer_key: All MA occurrences and billing details must live strictly under the MA record itself, not the origin Job or on an Estimate/Invoice record.
grades.student_response: scheduled work orders to ensure consistency in work for when the slower times come there are still recurring jobs and tasks to do that generate money on their own
grades.comment: Missing key points: under MA record itself, not on Estimate/Invoice

grades.question: Can multiple assets be listed on one invoice and billed together?
grades.answer_key: No.
grades.comment: Expected: no. Student answered: yes

grades.question: How can a technician review work order history for a specific asset?
grades.answer_key: From the Asset record, click the Related Jobs subtab.
grades.student_response: By viewing previous work done by other techs, notes done by admins and other service technicians
grades.comment: Partial answer. Has: Asset record. Missing: Related Jobs subtab

grades.question: Two ways GPS coordinates can be used to find exact locations?
grades.answer_key: 1) Asset record GPS coordinates - attach coordinates to an asset for navigation. 2) GPS coordinates in Address Line 1 of Customer record - for properties without conventional addresses.
grades.student_response:
Allowing managers to find the closest driver to a job site.
Asset Trackers utilize internal GPS antennas to monitor the exact location and status of equipment like generators or trailers via dynamic maps and historical activity report
grades.comment: Got some key points (1/3). Has: Asset record GPS coordinates. Missing: Address Line 1 of Customer record, two distinct methods

grades.question: Is Recurring Jobs standalone or always tied to Maintenance Agreement?
grades.answer_key: Yes, Recurring Jobs is a standalone feature. Recurring Billing houses Invoice records for Recurring Jobs. Maintenance Agreements house Job records and Invoice records separately.
grades.student_response: Yes , it's separate from maintenance agreements.
grades.comment: Missing key points: standalone feature, not always tied to MA

grades.question: Is ACH supported in Recurring Billing?
grades.answer_key: No.
grades.comment: Expected: no. Student answered: yes

grades.question: Do we currently have Recurring Billing templates?
grades.answer_key: No.

grades.question: Can Recurring Billing be created in the mobile app?
grades.answer_key: No.

grades.question: Do saved credit cards pull into Recurring Billing?
grades.answer_key: Yes.

grades.question: How can Material Lists be used? Give two examples.
grades.answer_key: 1) Go List/Checklist for technician to load truck with needed tools/materials. 2) Pre Estimate Step - add items to Material List for billing, inventory deducted at Completed status, then pull items into Estimate or Invoice.
key_points: Pre Estimate step for billing items
grades.student_response:
When you visit a job site, you can make a list of all the materials you’ll need to get the work done. This helps you know exactly what to buy. Or an alternative to this is your company creating an estimate first. The technician can then fill out the material list based on the materials needed to complete the work or for what they actually used during the job.
After you create the list, a colleague can check it. This list can then be used to create an estimate (a rough cost of the job) or an invoice (the final bill).
You can choose to include some or all of the materials from your list in the estimate or invoice so the customer knows exactly what they’re paying for.
grades.comment: Missing most key points: Go List/Checklist for loading truck, Pre Estimate step for billing items, two distinct examples

grades.question: What is a Line Item in FieldPulse?
grades.answer_key: A Line Item is anything customers need to complete a Job - parts, materials, labor and overhead.
grades.student_response: A line Item is a single product or service entry that appears on Estimates, Invoices, Purchase Orders, and related sales/inventory records.
grades.comment: Missing key points: parts, materials, labor, overhead, needed to complete a job

grades.question: How can we describe a Job/Service in terms of Estimate & Invoice creation?
grades.answer_key: A job/service is a collection of Line Items; what is needed to complete the job and charge the end customer.
key_points: what's needed to complete job and charge customer
grades.comment: No response provided

grades.question: What is the purpose of an Expiration Date on an Estimate?
grades.answer_key: Sets a time limit for how long quoted prices and terms are valid. Protects against price volatility. Encourages timely customer decisions.
grades.student_response: Sets the date after which pricing, terms, and availability are no longer guaranteed. Helps you avoid honoring old estimates when material/ labor costs or scope have changed.
grades.comment: Missing most key points: time limit for pricing, protects against price changes, encourages timely decisions

grades.question: What are Bundles? Where do I create them?
grades.answer_key: Bundles are created in Company Settings and group together services and parts commonly added together. Save time by adding multiple items at once. Next best thing if not ready for Pricebook.
grades.student_response: Invoice Bundles are saved groups of services and/or parts that you commonly use together on estimates and invoices. In the main menu, click “Company Settings” -> “Estimates and Invoices” -> “Invoice Bundles” -> “Create New”.
grades.comment: Partial answer. Has: Company Settings, group services and parts. Missing: save time, alternative to Pricebook

grades.question: How many Estimate options can be added?
grades.answer_key: 30 Estimate options, but best practice is 3 (Good, Better, Best).
grades.comment: Missing key points: 30, best practice 3

grades.question: When is it crucial for a Contract to be attached to an Estimate?
grades.answer_key: When the user is looking to collect a signature on the Estimate in person/face-to-face with their customer.
grades.student_response: Always crucial because you will need a customer to sign off on the estimate
grades.comment: Missing key points: collect signature, in person, face-to-face

grades.question: How many Contracts can be attached to an Estimate or Invoice?
grades.answer_key: Only one.
grades.comment: Partial answer. Has: one. Missing: 1

grades.question: Can every FieldPulse user request a down deposit on an Estimate?
grades.answer_key: MUST HAVE FIELDPULSE PAYMENTS ENABLED. On Draft Estimate click View Estimate, then Email to Customer, enter Payment Request Amount.
grades.comment: Missing most key points: must have FieldPulse Payments enabled, not every user, View Estimate, Email to Customer, Payment Request Amount

grades.question: Can a voice recording be attached to an Estimate or Invoice?
grades.answer_key: Yes.
grades.comment: Expected: yes. Student answered: no

grades.question: Can a user leverage different logos and branding from one Estimate to the next?
grades.answer_key: Yes.

grades.question: What limitations of child record estimate/invoice workflow cause problems?
grades.answer_key: The invoice remains tied to the child record, so you don't get true billing from parent to child. For proper parent-level billing the invoice must be created under the parent account.
key_points: invoice must be created under parent
grades.student_response: For many commercial and property-management customers, the simple "send to parent" function is insufficient because they require a system that can reliably manage specific billing needs: correctly identifying who receives the estimate or invoice (the dedicated billing or AP contact) beyond just listing where it's billed (the billing address); supporting different billing recipients for each property or location; and ensuring consistent routing of billing communications without depending on internal staff tribal knowledge.
grades.comment: Got some key points (1/3). Has: no true parent-to-child billing. Missing: invoice tied to child record, invoice must be created under parent

grades.question: Difference between standard and Serialized Inventory tracking?
grades.answer_key: Standard tracks quantities of identical items in bulk. Serialized tracks each individual item with a unique serial number. Serialized has preconfigured Asset templates where serial number auto-fills.
grades.student_response: Standard Inventory for high-volume consumables, tracking only total quantity and deducting stock automatically upon invoicing. For high-value or warranty-sensitive gear, Serialized Inventory provides unit-level traceability by requiring you to log and select unique serial numbers for each item sold. While Standard is best for low-risk stock, Serialized ensures precise tracking by removing specific units from your records only after a serial number is selected and payment is processed.
grades.comment: Missing key points: standard = bulk quantities, serialized has asset templates

grades.question: Can Custom Fields be added to Inventory Line Items? Give examples.
grades.answer_key: Yes! Examples: Aisle, Shelf, Bin Number.
grades.student_response: Yes. You can organize your stock by specifying the exact Aisle, Shelf, and Bin where each item is stored for precise location tracking.
grades.comment: Partial answer. Has: Yes. Missing: examples like Aisle/Shelf/Bin Number

grades.question: When does Inventory get deducted?
grades.answer_key: Users select deduction preference: Material List (at Completed status) or Invoice (at Invoiced/Finalized status).
grades.student_response: When a Material List is Completed or an Invoice is Invoiced/Finalized

grades.question: Can a user decide which hub Inventory deducts from?
grades.answer_key: No, Inventory deducted from the Material List or Invoice creator's assigned hub every time.
grades.comment: Expected: no. Student answered: yes

grades.question: Can FieldPulse support truck-swapping technicians for Inventory?
grades.answer_key: No, doesn't track cross-truck usage in real time. Would be a huge manual lift, should not recommend.
grades.comment: Expected: no. Student answered: yes

grades.question: How can supplier/vendor website links be saved for quick access?
grades.answer_key: Via Item List, add URL to Item Link section for each Line Item (Mobile) or click Add Link button next to Unit Cost (Web).
grades.student_response: You can save and access supplier information, including website links, through the Supplier Chat (powered by ProKeep) and Supplier Integrations features.
grades.comment: Missing key points: Item List, Item Link section, Add Link button

grades.question: Can FieldPulse validate inventory availability in real time?
grades.answer_key: No. Inventory levels are not checked in real time when adding items to estimates, invoices, or material lists.
grades.comment: Got yes/no right but could add more context: not checked in real time

grades.question: Best practice for reallocating materials after bulk shipment?
grades.answer_key: Manually move materials to trucks. Cannot have POs send inventory to multiple warehouses or trucks. Mobile: adjust quantities with +/- buttons per Hub. Web: similar manual adjustment.
grades.student_response: Our best practice recommendation is to receive everything into a central hub first, then manually reallocate counts to each truck hub. In the mobile app, you can manually adjust quantities. In the web app you can scan everything in and easily transfer within half
grades.comment: Missing most key points: manual process, cannot auto-distribute from POs, mobile +/- buttons, web adjustment

grades.question: How many suppliers/vendors on one PO?
grades.answer_key: Only one.
grades.student_response: On a single Purchase Order record in FieldPulse, you can list only one supplier/vendor.
grades.comment: Partial answer. Has: one. Missing: 1

grades.question: Does FieldPulse support partial receiving on a PO?
grades.answer_key: No!

grades.question: What is Supplier Chat? Value props?
grades.answer_key: Direct access to 8,500+ suppliers across 25 industries. Free feature. Can ask about inventory, pricing, order status, how to place an order. Can create PO and send via Supplier Chat. Available on web AND mobile.
grades.student_response: Supplier Chat is a built-in messaging tool in FieldPulse that provides real-time, direct access to over 8,500 suppliers across the U.S.
grades.comment: Missing most key points: 8500+ suppliers, 25 industries, free feature, inventory/pricing/orders, web and mobile, send POs

grades.question: What Commission structures are available?
grades.answer_key: Company Default Commission, User-Specific Commission, Flat Rate Job Commissions (Pricebook), Ad-Hoc Commission.
grades.student_response: FieldPulse tracks commissions through both system-generated and manual entries, ranging from Percentage of Gross Sales where payments are automatically calculated based on the invoice total to Flat-Rate amounts for specific jobs or upsells. Managers can also issue Spiffs or Bonuses, which are event-based incentives such as rewards for receiving five-star customer reviews. Ultimately, the system distinguishes between System Generated commissions, which follow predefined programs like membership sales, and Manual entries, which allow admins to key in ad-hoc, one-off bonuses or fixed dollar amounts.
grades.comment: Got the gist (2/4). Missing: Company Default, User-Specific

grades.question: Can Commission be split?
grades.answer_key: Yes, but at the moment this is a manual process, not automatic.
grades.comment: Expected: yes. Student answered: no

grades.question: Can FieldPulse run payroll or integrate with payroll software?
grades.answer_key: No. Can bring in time but that's it. Export time and use outside of platform.
grades.comment: Got yes/no right but could add more context: can export time

grades.question: Value props of Pricebook? How explain to prospect?
grades.answer_key: Simple tool for technicians to create estimates/invoices quickly using preset flat-rate items and bundles. Build custom estimate/invoice on-site with tiered choices (Good/Better/Best). Present for signature and payment. Makes upselling easier, reduces quoting time, helps close jobs in the field.
grades.student_response:
Pricebook is your ‘menu’ of services inside FieldPulse. Instead of building estimates line-by-line, your tech taps the job they’re doing—then FieldPulse auto-builds the estimate with your pricing, so every tech quotes the same way. It also makes upsells feel natural, because you can show Good/Better/Best options side-by-side. The customer chooses what they want, you collect a signature/payment, and you convert it straight into an estimate or invoice in a couple clicks.

Pricebook turns your service offerings into a sellable, profitable menu—so quoting is fast, pricing is consistent, and techs can easily present options.
grades.comment: Missing most key points: flat-rate items, tiered choices Good/Better/Best, upselling, reduces quoting time

grades.question: What does flat rate pricing mean?
grades.answer_key: One set price for a job or service instead of billing by hour or by each part. Customer knows total cost upfront.
grades.student_response: Flat Rate Pricing is a "value-based" billing method where you charge customers a single, fixed price for a specific service—such as $250 for a Water Heater Tune-Up—regardless of how long the job actually takes.
grades.comment: Missing key points: not hourly, customer knows cost upfront

grades.question: Can Price Tiers be managed through Pricebook?
grades.answer_key: No, Price Tiers are tied to Customer records not Pricebook.
grades.comment: Expected: no. Student answered: yes

grades.question: Can each Pricebook have different permissions per user?
grades.answer_key: No! Permissions cannot be set per pricebook. Access control applies to the Pricebook feature as a whole.
grades.comment: Got yes/no right but could add more context: feature-wide access control

grades.question: What industries utilize Pricebook the most?
grades.answer_key: HVAC, Plumbing, Garage Door
grades.comment: Got the gist (2/3). Missing: Garage Door

grades.question: Main differences between Wisetack and Acorn Financing?
grades.answer_key: Wisetack: 3.9% convenience fee, $500-$25,000 loan range, FieldPulse user must apply (US based, 1 year in business, $150K revenue, 10+ reviews at 4.5+ stars). Acorn: No application for FieldPulse user, end customer applies directly, Acorn wires funds to end customer, $1,000-$100,000 loan range.
key_points: Acorn: no application, end customer applies, $1K-100K, funds to end customer
grades.student_response: Acorn has a $0 cost to the contractor while Wisetack has a 3.9%, Also Acorn has a loan amount of 1,000 - 100,000 while Wisetack has a lone amount of 500 - 25,000.
grades.comment: Got some key points (1/2). Has: Wisetack: 3.9% fee, $500-25K, user must apply/qualify. Missing: Acorn: no application, end customer applies, $1K-100K, funds to end customer

grades.question: Which financing to recommend and when to offer?
grades.answer_key: Acorn Financing. Free for users, no application. 12+ lenders. Offer on Estimates (not Invoices) because funds take 1-2 business days. Customers may choose 'best' option since they have funding.
grades.student_response: We should recommend Acorn finance because it's the most beneficial for our customer due to the 0% fee to their Job that way they're getting 100% of their earnings. The reason why that's important is because other FSM/CRM charge anywhere between 8-10% of the contractor's job.
grades.comment: Got some key points (2/5). Has: Acorn, free. Missing: no application, offer on Estimates, funds take 1-2 days

grades.question: Can both Wisetack and Acorn be offered on Estimate records?
grades.answer_key: Nope! Must choose one or the other.
grades.comment: Got yes/no right but could add more context: choose one

grades.question: Response to customer wanting to keep their own financing company?
grades.answer_key: Wisetack and Acorn are the only native options. Can add a link to their existing provider in the default Estimate email body.
key_points: add link in Estimate email body
grades.student_response: You can have your own lender’s links, text, and terms manually into estimate templates, line items, or email template, but there is no true integrations in the platform
grades.comment: Got some key points (1/2). Has: add link in Estimate email body. Missing: only native options are Wisetack/Acorn

grades.student_response: Rainforest charges a 3% credit card fee,  ACH charges a 1% up to 99k

grades.student_response: Fees shift to 3.5% for next business days for credit cards and 1.5% for ACH

grades.question: What is Card Fee Recovery (CFR)?
grades.answer_key: A compliant dual pricing program that allows customers to pass on card processing fees to their customers.
grades.student_response: Card Fee Recovery is FieldPulse’s built-in way to help merchants recoup credit card processing costs in a transparent, compliant way without using a traditional surcharge.

grades.question: What does CFR look like in FieldPulse?
grades.answer_key: CFR dynamically increases line item price by chosen %. Invoice displays higher Total Card Price (with fees) and Total ACH/Cash Price (base price, since illegal to pass on ACH fees).
key_points: illegal to pass on ACH fees
grades.student_response: Card Fee Recovery mainly changes how invoices/estimates and payment screens look so the customer clearly sees a card price vs a cash/ACH price
grades.comment: Some overlap but missing major points: dynamically increases line item price, Total ACH/Cash Price, illegal to pass on ACH fees

grades.question: Can customer choose what % of fees to pass on with CFR?
grades.answer_key: Yes! Recommends 3%, but can pass on whatever they want.
grades.comment: Got yes/no right but could add more context: recommends 3%, flexible

grades.question: Fees with Card Fee Recovery?
grades.answer_key: 4.0% & 1.0% ACH (ACH fees cannot be passed on)
grades.student_response: For standard funding (Rainforest), the default fee distribution is 1% to the FieldPulse customer and 3% to the end customer. If Next-Day Funding is utilized, the default distribution changes to 1.5% for the FieldPulse customer and 3% for the end customer.
grades.comment: Missing key points: 4%, 1% ACH

grades.question: Fees with Next-Business Day Funding AND CFR?
grades.answer_key: 4.5% & 1.5% ACH (ACH fees cannot be passed on)
grades.student_response: Next-Day Funding is utilized, the default distribution changes to 1.5% for the FieldPulse customer and 3% for the end customer.
grades.comment: Partial answer. Has: 1.5% ACH. Missing: 4.5%

grades.question: Does Rainforest have automated monthly statements?

grades.comment: Got yes/no right but could add more context: FP Payments tab, dynamic reports, can export

grades.question: Is FieldPulse Payments PCI Compliant?

grades.question: Why is Reporting important?
grades.answer_key: Informed decision-making, performance tracking (KPIs), enhanced customer service, operational efficiency, financial management.
grades.student_response: This feature allows the customer to pull reports from important aspects of the business. The reason why it is important is because now our customers have the ability to fully see the reports from the raw data available within FieldPulse. This is important because now our customers have the ability to find inefficiencies within their business if there is any and also can see if there's any gaps within estimates, jobs, payments, timesheets, etc.
grades.comment: Missing most key points: decision-making, performance tracking, customer service, operational efficiency, financial management

grades.question: What user roles have access to Reporting?

grades.question: Is Reporting available in mobile app?

grades.question: How is a visual graph/chart created in Reporting?
grades.answer_key: Run report, highlight data, right click or double click, select Chart, make edits. Yes it can be added to Dashboard.
grades.student_response: To create visual charts, you must first generate a Raw Data report by navigating to Management → Reporting, picking a record type, choosing Raw Data (or Raw Data Beta), setting your date type + date range, and clicking Load Data. Once the data table is loaded, you can customize it using Filters, Groupings, and Columns. Next, highlight the cells/columns you want to visualize in the table, right-click on the highlighted area, hover over “Chart Range”, and select your visual report type. Finally, fine-tune the chart in the chart panel using the three tabs: Settings (chart type, color scheme), Data (categories/series inclusion/exclusion), and Format (fonts, legend, and other visual details). Yes it can be added to the dashboard as a widget.

grades.question: Can you create custom dashboards for different user roles?

grades.student_response: First you have to make sure you're tracking your call backs the right way, make sure they're their own style of workflow. Decide whether you want it as a site visit or job. then you can child a site visit based callback report.

grades.question: What can be done in Offline Mode?
grades.answer_key: Whole app is NOT accessible. Customers: cache related customers, access/edit 200 existing Custom Forms, create unlimited new draft forms. Jobs: cache 1 week, change status, upload 50 photos, save notes, access/edit 200 forms. Projects: cache 1 week, access/edit 200 forms. Custom Forms: up to 20 templates. Estimates & Invoices: cache 600 total, search 10K line items, view cached payments, preview finalized records, gather signatures, update status, 100 estimate/100 invoice templates. Cannot: collect payments, generate PDF. New drafts: can create linked to records, cannot preview/convert/collect signatures/payments/PDF.
grades.student_response:
Offline users can cache customers, and their related Jobs and Projects (Job/Project data caches for one week). For cached records (Jobs, Projects, and Customers), users can access/edit up to 200 existing Custom Forms each (600 total) and create unlimited new draft forms, plus access up to 20 Custom Form templates. Job activities supported offline include uploading up to 50 photos per Job, saving notes, and changing status. For Estimates and Invoices, up to 600 total cached records can be accessed, utilizing a cached Item List (up to 10,000 items), viewing payment records (read-only), previewing finalized records, collecting signatures (PDFs generated online), updating status, and accessing up to 100 Estimate and 100 Invoice templates. Users can also create new Estimate or Invoice drafts while offline, tied to related records. 

No, the entire application is not available.
grades.comment: Hit key points: 5/8

grades.question: What data syncs from QB Online? One way or two way?
grades.answer_key: Customers (two way), Line Items (two way), Estimates (two way), Invoices (two way), Payments (two way), Purchase Orders (two way), Vendors (two way)
grades.student_response: Synchronization between the two systems is primarily two-way for Customers, Estimates, Invoices, and Payments. Data that syncs only one-way, from QuickBooks Online (QBO) to FieldPulse, includes Products & Services (Items) and Projects (as related customers). Not synchronized at all are FieldPulse Projects/Jobs, extra customer and item metadata, invoice groupings/custom fields, and receipts.

grades.question: What data syncs from QB Desktop? One way or two way?
grades.answer_key: Customers (two way), Line Items (two way), Estimates (two way), Invoices (two way), Payments (two way), Purchase Orders (ONE WAY - FP to QB)
key_points: Purchase Orders ONE WAY FP to QB
grades.student_response: The FieldPulse and QuickBooks Desktop (QBD) integration primarily operates as a two-way sync for core financial and CRM data, including customers, line items, estimates, invoices, payments, vendors, and classes. This ensures that edits made in either system—such as updating a customer's address or adjusting a line item's price—reflect automatically in the other, though a one-way (FieldPulse to QBD) mode is available upon request for those who prefer more restricted data flow. While tax codes and single-level sub-customers also sync bidirectionally, certain specialized data remains one-way: timesheets push exclusively from FieldPulse to QuickBooks Time, and purchase orders currently default to a FieldPulse-to-QBD push, though enhanced two-way capabilities are becoming available.

ungraded_responses.question: What reminders and notifications can we provide?
ungraded_responses.response: The FieldPulse platform utilizes several main communication channels, including Email for job and appointment confirmations/reminders, estimate and invoice send/reminder emails, Assignment User job assignment notifications, and payment events like chargeback alerts. SMS is used for appointment reminders and some booking confirmations and customer updates when enabled. In-app & push notifications deliver user reminders, task follow-ups, and job assignment/updates when a user logs into the mobile or web application. Finally, Fleet-specific alerts, such as geofence and maintenance alerts, are delivered via email, SMS, or in-app notifications through the dedicated fleet system.

grades.question: What are the different user roles within FieldPulse?
grades.answer_key: Admin, Team Manager, Service Agent, Limited Agent, Assignment User
grades.student_response: Administrator, team manager, service agent, limited agent, assignment user

grades.question: At a high level, how would you describe how each of these user roles differ?
grades.answer_key: Admin users have access to everything. Team Managers and Service Agents have access to web & mobile app with configurable permissions toggled on/off by admins. Limited Agents can only access mobile app, can only upload files, use timesheets, be assigned to a team, can only see jobs directly assigned to them. Assignment Users have no access to web or mobile app, can be assigned to jobs/tasks/subtasks, receive email when assigned.
key_points: Team Manager/Service Agent = web & mobile with configurable permissions
Limited Agent = mobile only, limited visibility
Assignment User = no app access, email notifications only
grades.student_response:
Admin - you have full access to the FP account and full visibility into the business, along with full functionality

Team Manager - can be assigned to lead multiple teams, create and assign work orders to their specific team members with sales functionality (creating estimates/invoices, utilizing pricebooks, purchase orders, etc.)

Service agent - designed for techs out in the field and their permission settings can be adjusted as desired. Can view customer profiles, jobs assigned to themselves, have sales functionality (if you'd like), clock in/out, add notes, update status workflows, etc.

Limited agent - can see the work assigned to them via the mobile app only. Can be assigned to a team but great for those who don't need to do much within FP -- see the jobs assigned to them specifically, upload files, clock in/out

Assignment user - they will receive emails outlining the job details they are assigned to, but do not have access to FP app or the ability to use any of its functionality

grades.student_response:
Parent/child: parent is the primary, top-level account (corporate office, property mgmt company, or billing entity). Child is the individual service location, unit, or customer being serviced -- this allows for job specific billing flexibility

Multi-location/multi-contact: all services are tracked under one customer account, but this allows you to add multiple service addresses to a single primary customer record -- typical when a single customer has multiple locations (EX: commercial businesses)


Primary difference is billing hierarchy and QB sync

grades.section: General Knowledge: Projects, Jobs, Site Visits, & Tasks
grades.question: What is the difference between Projects, Jobs, and Site Visits?
grades.answer_key: Projects: folder that stores/organizes Job & Site Visit records, for long term work. Jobs: standalone or part of a Project, for work typically completed that day (standalone) or over several days/weeks (part of project). Site Visits: subcomponent of a Job record, for callbacks/go backs/return visits.
key_points: Jobs = standalone or part of project
Site Visits = subcomponent of Job for callbacks/return visits
grades.student_response:
Projects -- for jobs that will span multiple weeks or months with multiple team members involved, specific timelines, etc.

Jobs -- individual jobs or work orders

Site visits -- any additional visits for a job already scheduled/occurring
grades.comment: Got some key points (1/3). Has: Site Visits = subcomponent of Job for callbacks/return visits. Missing: Projects = folder/organizer for long-term work, Jobs = standalone or part of project

grades.section: General Knowledge: Projects, Jobs, Site Visits, & Tasks
grades.question: What are the benefits of using Site Visit records instead of creating a brand new Job?
grades.answer_key: Quicker creation, directly attached to the primary work order, all information under one primary record. Don't have to duplicate a record leaving half info in one job and half in another.
grades.student_response: Rather than having to create a new job record for each visit you are doing, you can easily create site visits under that one job record to keep things organized and streamlined all under that one specific job record and you will be able to see the entire history for that job
grades.comment: Missing most key points: quicker creation, attached to primary work order, no duplication

grades.section: General Knowledge: Projects, Jobs, Site Visits, & Tasks
grades.question: If you hear 'Call Back/Go Back/Follow Up' what record type do you recommend?
grades.answer_key: Site Visit

Field Notes = technician notes from the field

Job notes - job notes on customer record that are automatically reinserted onto job records for that customer

Field notes - notes left by the assigned user(s) on that specific job

grades.question: What is a Task in FieldPulse? Give 3 examples.

grades.question: ClearPath pitch in 4 sentences or less
grades.answer_key: With ClearPath, you, as a business owner, can customize the instructions and actions available to your technicians during each step of their workflow. This ensures that your technicians no longer question what to do or where to go within the mobile app; everything they need will be on one main screen.
grades.student_response: With ClearPath, you can set up specific actions and steps for your techs to follow at each step of your custom workflows to act as guardrails to ensure they are completing the job to your required level of service every single time, but also with no questions or confusion on their end. Overall, it will give you peace of mind as a business owner that the job is getting completed the right way every time with more consistency, but also will make it super simple for the techs so they can focus on completing the actual job with a clear outline
grades.comment: Missing most key points: customize instructions/actions, technicians know what to do, mobile app, one main screen

grades.question: List at least 3 value props of ClearPath.
grades.answer_key: Consistency for customers because each tech completes job same way; save time because guesswork removed; easier onboarding of new team members; save time with templates for Estimates and Forms; save time with default prepopulated text for comments.
grades.student_response:
1) they will have peace of mind knowing that each job is getting completed not only the right way they would do it, but the way their best tech would do it every time

2) it makes things very simple and easy for the team out in the field -- basically making it "dummy proof" for techs that aren't always the most tech savvy

3) this will save the tech's time so they can complete jobs more quickly, and also for any techs hired in the future, it will be that much smoother for them to learn and get adapted to the platform
grades.comment: Missing most key points: consistency, save time/remove guesswork, easier onboarding, templates, prepopulated text

grades.question: Describe the relationship between Job Custom Status Workflows, Status Action Flows, and Focus View
grades.answer_key: Job Custom Status Workflows are the high-level Statuses common to a type of work. Status Action Flows allow the business to outline the steps and instructions for each specific job they perform (e.g., Shower Installation vs Toilet Installation). Focus View provides the custom mobile experience that neatly displays all of this information for each technician.
key_points: Status Action Flows = specific steps/instructions per job type
grades.student_response:
Job custom status workflows -- job-specific workflows that are customizable with specific stages to follow for that job or project

Status action flows (the blueprint) -- specific for ClearPath. these steps and statuses will guide the technicians with specific actions/steps to that job or service and provide clear instructions at each step of the workflow

Focus view (the guided experience) -- also specific for ClearPath -- this is a mobile-first view designed to give your techs guard rails to make sure each job is getting completed how you'd want it to every single time

grades.question: Are Action Buttons, Status Instructions, and Widgets all required at all times?
grades.answer_key: Nope! A customer can choose to just use Action Buttons and/or Status Instructions OR take it further by enabling Focus View and utilizing Widgets.
grades.comment: Got yes/no right but could add more context: can choose which to use, Widgets require Focus View

grades.question: Are technicians always locked into Focus View?
grades.answer_key: You can decide on a per Status basis when you want technicians to be locked into Focus View.
grades.student_response: no, they're not always locked into focus view if you don't want them to be but when they are locked into focus view, it will be locked in the entire time -- not at specific statuses versus others
grades.comment: Partial answer. Has: No. Missing: per Status basis

grades.student_response: Jobs, projects, material lists, invoices & estimates

grades.question: What are the benefits of some Status types in Custom Status Workflows?

grades.student_response: Automated communications (EX: sending ETA to customers), process alignment, time tracking/analytics, focused views, task coordination, workflow validation
grades.comment: Missing key points: Travel Time tracks time, In Progress tracks time, Pending pauses time tracking

grades.answer_key: A 'New' and 'Completed' type of status are required.

From there, you can filter out jobs associated with a maintenance agreement

grades.student_response: We do not have an AI or automated route optimization feature in FieldPulse
grades.comment: Missing key points: Schedule Route or Reassign Jobs, map view, manual

grades.question: What is an Asset in FieldPulse? List 2 examples.

grades.student_response: With asset management in FieldPulse, you will be able to manage and keep track of any equipment -- examples included but not limited to: generators, furnaces, electrical panels, etc. You can see model name, warranty information, install dates, and more

grades.question: Why should we showcase Assets before jumping into Maintenance Agreements?

grades.question: What is a Maintenance Agreement in FieldPulse?

grades.student_response: Simple way to keep track of any maintenance or recurring work with internal and external reminders for customers -- can also create tiered maintenance plans on a silver, gold, or platinum price scale

grades.answer_key: Sets a time limit for how long quoted prices and terms are valid. Protects against price volatility. Encourages timely customer decisions.

key_points: invoice must be created under parent

grades.comment: Missing most key points: invoice tied to child record, no true parent-to-child billing, invoice must be created under parent

grades.answer_key: Standard tracks quantities of identical items in bulk. Serialized tracks each individual item with a unique serial number. Serialized has preconfigured Asset templates where serial number auto-fills.

Standard: bulk quantity tracking -- montiors the total number of units available in a hub

Serialized: unit level tracking, each unit is assigned a unique serial number

EX: item condition, related job

HOW:

grades.question: What is Supplier Chat? Value props?

grades.question: Value props of Pricebook? How explain to prospect?
grades.answer_key: Simple tool for technicians to create estimates/invoices quickly using preset flat-rate items and bundles. Build custom estimate/invoice on-site with tiered choices (Good/Better/Best). Present for signature and payment. Makes upselling easier, reduces quoting time, helps close jobs in the field.
grades.student_response: Pricebooks are a flat-rate sales tool that customers are able to utilize to easily help your techs sell more, automate the estimate/invoice creation process in just a few seconds, and overall just helps to simplify the workday down to a few seconds while providing a solid experience for your customers as well
grades.comment: Missing most key points: tiered choices Good/Better/Best, upselling, reduces quoting time, close in field

grades.question: What does flat rate pricing mean?

grades.question: Main differences between Wisetack and Acorn Financing?
grades.answer_key: Wisetack: 3.9% convenience fee, $500-$25,000 loan range, FieldPulse user must apply (US based, 1 year in business, $150K revenue, 10+ reviews at 4.5+ stars). Acorn: No application for FieldPulse user, end customer applies directly, Acorn wires funds to end customer, $1,000-$100,000 loan range.
key_points: Acorn: no application, end customer applies, $1K-100K, funds to end customer
grades.student_response:
Wisetack: you have to qualify for WiseTack, anything between $500 - $25k you can have financing appear, they take 3.9% and they collect money over time from the customer but you are paid upfront upon completion

Acorn: complete free for all FieldPulse users, everyone qualifies, no fees - applies to jobs from $1k - $100k

grades.question: Which financing to recommend and when to offer?
grades.answer_key: Acorn Financing. Free for users, no application. 12+ lenders. Offer on Estimates (not Invoices) because funds take 1-2 business days. Customers may choose 'best' option since they have funding.
grades.student_response:
Acorn because there are no fees for them and everyone qualifies

Users should offer this to end customers on estimates rather than invoices. This allows customers to choose higher-tier project options because they have secured funding, and accounts for the 1-2 business days required for funds to be deposited
grades.comment: Hit key points: 3/5

grades.question: Can both Wisetack and Acorn be offered on Estimate records?
grades.answer_key: Nope! Must choose one or the other.
grades.comment: Got yes/no right but could add more context: choose one

grades.question: Response to customer wanting to keep their own financing company?
grades.answer_key: Wisetack and Acorn are the only native options. Can add a link to their existing provider in the default Estimate email body.
key_points: add link in Estimate email body
grades.student_response: You would have to turn off the financing option within FieldPulse so cannot integrate, but you can technically add the link to your financing company for customers to follow into the "comments" section on the estimate or invoice or in the footnotes
grades.comment: Got some key points (1/2). Has: add link in Estimate email body. Missing: only native options are Wisetack/Acorn

grades.question: Who are our 2 main Fintech payment partners?

grades.question: Standard FieldPulse Payments (Rainforest) fees?

grades.question: What is Card Fee Recovery (CFR)?
grades.answer_key: A compliant dual pricing program that allows customers to pass on card processing fees to their customers.
grades.student_response: compliant dual-pricing program that allows merchants to recover card processing fees by presenting a higher Total Card Price and a lower Total Cash/ACH Price upfront

grades.question: What does CFR look like in FieldPulse?
grades.answer_key: CFR dynamically increases line item price by chosen %. Invoice displays higher Total Card Price (with fees) and Total ACH/Cash Price (base price, since illegal to pass on ACH fees).
key_points: illegal to pass on ACH fees
grades.student_response: learly displays processing costs on invoices/estimates, allowing customers to choose their preferred payment method
grades.comment: Missing most key points: dynamically increases line item price, Total Card Price, Total ACH/Cash Price, illegal to pass on ACH fees

grades.question: Can customer choose what % of fees to pass on with CFR?
grades.answer_key: Yes! Recommends 3%, but can pass on whatever they want.
grades.comment: Got yes/no right but could add more context: recommends 3%, flexible

grades.question: Fees with Card Fee Recovery?
grades.answer_key: 4.0% & 1.0% ACH (ACH fees cannot be passed on)
grades.student_response: Online Card: 4% + $0.25 per transaction & ACH Transactions: 1% up to $99,999.00

grades.question: Fees with Next-Business Day Funding AND CFR?

grades.question: What can be done in Offline Mode?
grades.answer_key: Whole app is NOT accessible. Customers: cache related customers, access/edit 200 existing Custom Forms, create unlimited new draft forms. Jobs: cache 1 week, change status, upload 50 photos, save notes, access/edit 200 forms. Projects: cache 1 week, access/edit 200 forms. Custom Forms: up to 20 templates. Estimates & Invoices: cache 600 total, search 10K line items, view cached payments, preview finalized records, gather signatures, update status, 100 estimate/100 invoice templates. Cannot: collect payments, generate PDF. New drafts: can create linked to records, cannot preview/convert/collect signatures/payments/PDF.
grades.student_response:
No, the whole mobile app is not accessible

What can be done:
Documentation: Save job notes, upload up to 50 photos at once, and complete forms
Records: Create new estimates, invoices, and material lists, which are saved as "Offline Drafts"
Signatures: Collect and save acceptance signatures to sync later
Caching: Access cached jobs, projects, and customers for up to one week
Status Updates: Change job, estimate, or invoice statuses

What is NOT accessible:
Real-time logic: ClearPath workflows, automation, and step-based logic
Financial tools: Loading the Pricebook or converting estimates to invoices
Previews: Previewing new estimate drafts or generating PDFs
Payments: Collecting payments requires an active connection

grades.question: What is 10DLC compliance?

grades.student_response: a US carrier registration system that requires businesses to register their brand and messaging campaigns before sending SMS to U.S. numbers
grades.comment: Missing most key points: industry-wide, mobile carriers, reduce spam, protect consumers

grades.question: What is the two-step 10DLC compliance process?
grades.answer_key: 1) Brand Registration: submitting business details. 2) Campaign Registration: publishing SMS compliance pages on user's website (Facebook groups not accepted).
grades.student_response:
Brand registration - customer submits business details, including legal name, EIN, address, website, and contact information

Campaign registration - customer must publish two publicly accessible compliance pages on their own website domain and provide the URLs to Engage

grades.question: What data cannot sync from QB Desktop?
grades.answer_key: Customer names >31 chars, special non-ASCII characters, line item names >31 chars, sales receipts, shipping fees, payments on estimates, additional payment methods beyond cash/check/credits/card/ACH, subclasses.
grades.student_response:
Subclasses: Only top-level classes sync  

Tax Codes: Multiple tax codes are not supported  

Estimates: Payments made on estimates will not sync and instead appear as "undeposited funds"  

Line Items: Negative line items (discounts), credit memos, and sales receipts are not supported  

Payment Methods: Any methods other than cash, check, credit card, or ACH (e.g., gift cards)  

Customer Details: Job history, locations, additional contacts, tags, custom fields, and lead sources  

Attachments: Images, files, and maintenance agreements  

Company Names: Only individual names sync; company name fields will be empty
grades.comment: Got some key points (3/6). Has: sales receipts, payments on estimates, subclasses. Missing: 31 char limit, special characters, shipping fees

grades.question: What data cannot sync from QB Online?
grades.answer_key: Customers: additional contacts/locations/custom fields/tags/files. Line Items: tags/UPC barcode/links/suppliers. Invoicing: grouping/custom fields/contracts/deposit field/shipping fees/surcharges. Payments: journal entries/refunds/voiding payments/custom payment types. Others: QB receipts/grouping/FP Projects or Jobs.
grades.student_response:
Projects: QBO projects do not sync to FieldPulse by default . If enabled, they sync only as related customers; projects created in FieldPulse will not sync back to QBO  

Customer Records: Records without a first name, last name, or company name will appear blank in FieldPulse  


Addresses: FieldPulse uses the QBO "Shipping Address" as the service address; if only a "Billing Address" exists, no address will display in FieldPulse  

Line Items: Items without a quantity and price in QBO will sync as $0 in FieldPulse  

Historical Data: If you have been duplicating efforts by creating the same invoices/estimates in both systems simultaneously, you should not sync that history
grades.comment: Missing most key points: additional contacts/locations, custom fields, tags, UPC barcode, grouping, contracts, shipping fees, journal entries, refunds

grades.question: When does a Customer record sync to QB?
grades.answer_key: Customers are the first to sync once initiated. Helps track how customers were brought in.
grades.student_response:
Customer records sync from FieldPulse to QuickBooks Online automatically when an invoice is marked as Invoiced or has a payment applied. You can also manually sync a record by clicking Sync with QuickBooks on the customer's profile 



This timing is important for lead and prospect management because:
Data Cleanliness: It prevents QuickBooks from being cluttered with leads or prospects who have not yet committed to a financial transaction  

Accounting Accuracy: It ensures that only customers with actual financial activity (invoices or payments) are created in your accounting "source of truth"  

Operational Efficiency: Your office team can manage the early stages of the sales funnel in FieldPulse without creating redundant or premature records in your accounting software
grades.comment: Partial answer. Has: first to sync. Missing: helps track customer acquisition

grades.question: Is a QB sync the same as a data migration?

key_points: migration = paid service with more detail
grades.comment: Missing key points: sync = basic data transfer, migration = paid service with more detail

grades.question: What info is generally needed for a data migration?
grades.answer_key: Access to prospect's current FSM or client to provide exports of desired data.
grades.student_response:
Customer Data Needs: Identify if the requirement is for a standard Data Transfer (QuickBooks sync, assets, custom fields) or a Full Data Migration (historical job details, estimates, invoices)  

Provider Extraction Requirements: Access to data varies by platform

Critical Documentation: A signed Data Migration Agreement and an updated Salesforce Migration Case are mandatory  

Sample Data: A meaningful sample size of data must be provided for review and customer sign-off before the full migration  

Mapping and Scope: Specific mapping for images (linked to Job IDs) and a clear plan for how estimates and invoices will be imported (e.g., as single line items
grades.comment: Missing key points: access to current FSM, data exports
